CodeDeploy handles application deployment, not configuration management. CloudFormation cfn-init provides some bootstrapping but is not a full Chef environment. Elastic Beanstalk uses its own configuration format (.ebextensions), not Chef.

quiz.question: A startup's engineering team is comfortable with Python and wants to define AWS infrastructure using familiar programming language constructs (loops, conditionals, classes) instead of YAML templates. Which AWS tool best supports this approach?
options: AWS CloudFormation with JSON templates
AWS SAM with YAML templates
AWS CDK with Python
AWS CodeDeploy with appspec.yml
quiz.explanation: AWS CDK (Cloud Development Kit) allows developers to define cloud infrastructure using general-purpose programming languages like Python, TypeScript, Java, and others, synthesizing into CloudFormation templates. CloudFormation and SAM both use declarative YAML/JSON, not imperative programming constructs. CodeDeploy's appspec.yml defines deployment behavior, not infrastructure provisioning.

quiz.question: A company needs to deploy a Docker-based application to an existing Kubernetes cluster running on their on-premises hardware. They require AWS support for cluster lifecycle management. Which EKS option should they choose?
options: EKS Distro — run the open-source EKS distribution on-premises without AWS support
EKS Anywhere — run EKS-compatible Kubernetes on-premises with AWS support
EKS on Fargate — serverless Kubernetes managed by AWS
EKS on EC2 — run worker nodes in a VPC managed by the team
quiz.explanation: EKS Anywhere is explicitly designed for running Kubernetes on-premises with AWS support for installation, upgrades, and troubleshooting. EKS Distro is the open-source version of Kubernetes used in EKS but comes WITHOUT AWS support. EKS Fargate and EKS on EC2 both run in AWS regions, not on-premises hardware.

quiz.question: A financial services company is evaluating IaC tools. They need a solution that: (1) supports multi-account deployments, and (2) can preview changes before applying them. Which TWO CloudFormation features directly address these requirements? (Choose TWO)
options: CloudFormation Nested Stacks
CloudFormation StackSets
CloudFormation Change Sets
CloudFormation Stack Policies
CloudFormation Rollback Configuration
quiz.explanation: StackSets enable deploying stacks across multiple AWS accounts and regions, addressing requirement 1. Change Sets provide a dry-run preview showing what will change before execution, addressing requirement 2. Nested Stacks manage template modularity within one account. Stack Policies control which resources can be updated but don't preview changes. Rollback Configuration monitors deployments and triggers rollbacks on failure.

quiz.question: A team is considering AWS CodeDeploy to automate their release process. A junior engineer suggests using CodeDeploy to provision a new RDS database as part of the deployment pipeline. Which statement is correct?
options: CodeDeploy can provision RDS instances if the appspec.yml includes CloudFormation references
CodeDeploy cannot provision new AWS resources; it only deploys application code to existing compute resources
CodeDeploy can provision RDS using deployment lifecycle hooks with AWS CLI commands
CodeDeploy supports resource provisioning only for EC2 instances, not RDS
quiz.explanation: CodeDeploy is strictly a code deployment service that deploys application artifacts to EXISTING compute resources (EC2, ECS, Lambda, on-premises). It does NOT provision any AWS resources. While lifecycle hooks can run scripts, using them to provision infrastructure is an anti-pattern and outside CodeDeploy's design scope. CloudFormation is the correct tool for provisioning RDS.

quiz.question: A company wants to run EKS workloads on AWS Outposts hardware located in their own data center to meet low-latency requirements for local applications. Which EKS deployment option supports this?
options: EKS Anywhere
EKS Distro
EKS on Outposts
EKS on Fargate
quiz.explanation: EKS on Outposts runs the EKS control plane or worker nodes on AWS Outposts racks physically located in the customer's data center, providing low-latency access to local applications while remaining AWS-managed. EKS Anywhere runs on non-AWS infrastructure (VMware, bare metal) with AWS support but is not Outposts. EKS Distro is the open-source distribution. EKS Fargate is serverless in AWS regions only.

quiz.question: A company is migrating from manually provisioned infrastructure to IaC. They need a solution that uses YAML/JSON templates and groups related AWS resources together as a single unit that can be created, updated, and deleted atomically. Which THREE CloudFormation concepts are most relevant to their architecture? (Choose THREE)
options: Stack — a group of AWS resources managed as a single unit
Nested Stack — parent stacks referencing child stacks for modularity
StackSet — deploying stacks across multiple accounts/regions
Change Set — previewing proposed changes before applying them
CodeDeploy Deployment Group — grouping EC2 instances for application deployment
quiz.explanation: A Stack is the fundamental CloudFormation unit — all resources in a template are managed atomically together. Nested Stacks provide modular architecture by allowing parent stacks to reference child stacks for loosely coupled tiers. Change Sets allow the team to preview and validate changes safely as they transition to IaC. StackSets are for multi-account scenarios (not the primary migration need here). CodeDeploy Deployment Groups are unrelated to infrastructure provisioning.

quiz.question: A machine learning team wants to run Kubernetes-based training jobs but needs complete control over the Kubernetes distribution to customize networking plugins and audit configurations. They plan to run this entirely on their own hardware with NO AWS support contract. Which option gives them the MOST flexibility with lowest cost?
options: EKS Anywhere, which provides AWS support with customizable configurations
EKS Distro, which is the open-source Kubernetes distribution used in EKS without AWS support
EKS on EC2, which provides full control over node configuration in AWS
EKS on Fargate, which removes all infrastructure management overhead
quiz.explanation: EKS Distro is the open-source Kubernetes distribution that teams can download, run on any infrastructure, and fully customize — with no AWS support or costs. It's ideal when teams want EKS-compatible Kubernetes with full control and no support overhead. EKS Anywhere runs on-premises with AWS support (adds cost). EKS EC2 and Fargate run in AWS regions, not on customer hardware.

quiz.question: A company manages a three-tier application using separate CloudFormation templates for the network layer, application layer, and database layer. They want to ensure that updating the application layer template does not accidentally affect the network layer resources. Which CloudFormation feature prevents unauthorized updates to specific stack resources?
options: Change Set — reviews proposed changes before execution
Stack Policy — defines which resources can be updated or replaced during stack updates
Rollback Triggers — automatically reverts stacks when CloudWatch alarms fire
Nested Stack — isolates each tier in its own stack
quiz.explanation: Stack Policies define explicit allow/deny rules for which resources within a stack can be updated or replaced during stack updates, preventing accidental modification of critical resources. Change Sets preview changes but don't prevent them from being applied. Rollback Triggers react to alarm conditions during deployments. Nested Stacks provide modular organization but don't enforce update restrictions on individual resources.

lessons.title: AWS Monitoring Services
lessons.overview: Understand Amazon CloudWatch and its sub-services (Metrics, Logs, Alarms, Events, Dashboards) for monitoring AWS infrastructure, and distinguish the three AWS health visibility tools: Service Health Dashboard, Personal Health Dashboard, and Health API.
lessons.overviewVi: Tìm hiểu Amazon CloudWatch và các dịch vụ con (Metrics, Logs, Alarms, Events, Dashboards) để giám sát hạ tầng AWS, và phân biệt 3 công cụ theo dõi sức khỏe: Service Health Dashboard, Personal Health Dashboard và Health API.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Why Monitoring Matters
sections.contentHtml:
Monitoring provides visibility into how your systems perform and helps forecast issues before they occur. AWS monitoring services can check CPU utilization, disk usage, network performance, application logs, and other metrics — without you having to manually log into servers.

Services coveredAmazon CloudWatch (Metrics, Logs, Alarms, Events, Dashboards), AWS Service Health Dashboard, AWS Personal Health Dashboard, AWS Health API
sections.contentVi: Monitoring cung cấp khả năng nhìn thấy hiệu suất hệ thống và giúp dự báo sự cố trước khi xảy ra. Các dịch vụ monitoring AWS có thể kiểm tra CPU, disk, network, application logs — không cần đăng nhập thủ công vào server. Các dịch vụ trong bài: Amazon CloudWatch (Metrics, Logs, Alarms, Events, Dashboards), AWS Service Health Dashboard, AWS Personal Health Dashboard, AWS Health API.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon CloudWatch Suite
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon CloudWatch is a suite of monitoring services for AWS and on-premises systems. It's a metrics repository that collects data from AWS services and custom applications, analyzes them, and notifies you when thresholds are reached.

CloudWatch MetricsCollects metrics from AWS services and custom apps. Most services send data every 1 minute; Amazon EC2 sends every 5 minutes by default (enable detailed monitoring for 1-minute EC2 metrics)
CloudWatch LogsMonitor, store, and access log files from AWS services or custom apps. Install the CloudWatch Logs Agent on EC2 to auto-collect and publish application logs
CloudWatch AlarmsSet threshold-based alarms on metrics. Triggers: Amazon SNS notification, EC2 Auto Scaling, billing alert, or Lambda invocation
CloudWatch EventsMonitors and responds to AWS service events in near real-time. Create rules to trigger actions on state changes or on a schedule (e.g., invoke Lambda every hour)
CloudWatch DashboardsCustomizable web console dashboards to monitor resources in a single view across different AWS regions; supports custom metrics
Exam tip: EC2 default = 5-minute metrics. Enable detailed monitoring to get 1-minute granularity.
sections.contentVi: Amazon CloudWatch là bộ dịch vụ monitoring cho AWS và on-premises. Đây là kho lưu trữ metrics, thu thập dữ liệu từ AWS services và ứng dụng tùy chỉnh, phân tích và thông báo khi ngưỡng đạt đến. CloudWatch Metrics: thu thập metrics từ AWS services và ứng dụng. Hầu hết gửi data mỗi 1 phút; Amazon EC2 mặc định gửi mỗi 5 phút (bật detailed monitoring để có 1-phút). CloudWatch Logs: monitor, lưu trữ và truy cập log từ AWS services hoặc ứng dụng tùy chỉnh; cài CloudWatch Logs Agent trên EC2 để tự động thu thập log. CloudWatch Alarms: đặt alarm theo ngưỡng metric. Kích hoạt: thông báo Amazon SNS, EC2 Auto Scaling, billing alert, hoặc gọi Lambda. CloudWatch Events: theo dõi và phản ứng với sự kiện AWS gần thời gian thực; tạo rule để trigger action khi trạng thái thay đổi hoặc theo lịch (ví dụ: gọi Lambda mỗi giờ). CloudWatch Dashboards: dashboard tùy chỉnh trên web console, xem resource trong một view qua nhiều AWS region; hỗ trợ custom metrics. Lưu ý thi: EC2 mặc định = metrics 5 phút; bật detailed monitoring để có 1 phút.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: AWS Health Dashboards and Health API
sections.contentHtml:
AWS Service Health DashboardPublic dashboard showing real-time availability status of ALL AWS services across all regions. Subscribe via RSS feed to get notified of service interruptions
AWS Personal Health DashboardPersonalized — shows only the AWS services you are using in your account. Shows health events (scheduled maintenance, infrastructure changes) that may affect your applications. Create alerts triggered by health changes
AWS Health APIProgrammatic access (RESTful HTTPS, JSON responses) to the same data in your Personal Health Dashboard. Requires a Business or Enterprise support plan — not available by default
Service Health vs Personal Health: Service Health = all AWS services (public); Personal Health = only YOUR services (personalized, account-scoped).
sections.contentVi: AWS Service Health Dashboard: dashboard công khai hiển thị trạng thái khả dụng thời gian thực của TẤT CẢ dịch vụ AWS trên mọi region. Đăng ký RSS feed để nhận thông báo khi gián đoạn dịch vụ. AWS Personal Health Dashboard: dashboard cá nhân hóa — chỉ hiển thị các dịch vụ AWS BẠN đang sử dụng trong account. Hiển thị health events (bảo trì định kỳ, thay đổi hạ tầng) có thể ảnh hưởng ứng dụng của bạn; tạo alert khi health thay đổi. AWS Health API: truy cập lập trình (RESTful HTTPS, response JSON) vào dữ liệu trong Personal Health Dashboard của bạn. Yêu cầu gói hỗ trợ Business hoặc Enterprise — mặc định không có sẵn. Phân biệt: Service Health = tất cả dịch vụ AWS (công khai); Personal Health = CHỈ dịch vụ bạn dùng (cá nhân hóa theo account).

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Monitoring: Key Exam Points
sections.contentHtml:
• EC2 default metric interval = 5 minutes; enable detailed monitoring for 1-minute granularity
  • CloudWatch Alarms notify via SNS; can also trigger Auto Scaling or invoke Lambda
  • CloudWatch Events = near real-time event response + scheduled jobs (not the same as Alarms)
  • CloudWatch Logs Agent on EC2 = automatically collects and publishes application logs to CloudWatch
  • Service Health Dashboard = ALL AWS services, public; subscribe via RSS
  • Personal Health Dashboard = your account's services only, shows maintenance affecting you
  • AWS Health API = requires Business or Enterprise support plan
Exam trap: If a question asks about monitoring a specific account's resource health events → Personal Health Dashboard. If it asks about overall AWS service availability → Service Health Dashboard.
sections.contentVi: Điểm thi quan trọng: EC2 default interval = 5 phút; bật detailed monitoring để có 1 phút. CloudWatch Alarms thông báo qua SNS; cũng có thể trigger Auto Scaling hoặc gọi Lambda. CloudWatch Events = phản ứng sự kiện gần thời gian thực + scheduled jobs (khác với Alarms). CloudWatch Logs Agent trên EC2 = tự động thu thập và publish application logs. Service Health Dashboard = TẤT CẢ dịch vụ AWS, công khai; đăng ký RSS. Personal Health Dashboard = chỉ dịch vụ của account bạn, hiển thị bảo trì ảnh hưởng đến bạn. AWS Health API = yêu cầu gói Business hoặc Enterprise. Bẫy thi: câu hỏi về giám sát health events của resource trong account → Personal Health Dashboard; về khả dụng dịch vụ AWS tổng thể → Service Health Dashboard.

summary: Amazon CloudWatch is a metrics repository suite: Metrics (collect data), Logs (store/query logs), Alarms (threshold notifications via SNS/scaling/Lambda), Events (near real-time event rules + scheduling), Dashboards (cross-region views)
EC2 sends CloudWatch metrics every 5 minutes by default — enable detailed monitoring to get 1-minute granularity
AWS Service Health Dashboard = public, shows ALL AWS services status across regions; subscribe to RSS for interruption alerts
AWS Personal Health Dashboard = personalized, shows only services you use in your account plus maintenance events affecting your resources
AWS Health API provides programmatic JSON/HTTPS access to Personal Health Dashboard data; requires Business or Enterprise support plan
summaryVi: Amazon CloudWatch là bộ metrics repository: Metrics (thu thập), Logs (lưu trữ/truy vấn log), Alarms (thông báo ngưỡng qua SNS/scaling/Lambda), Events (rule theo sự kiện thời gian thực + lịch), Dashboards (xem đa region)
EC2 gửi CloudWatch metrics mỗi 5 phút mặc định — bật detailed monitoring để có 1 phút
AWS Service Health Dashboard = công khai, hiển thị trạng thái TẤT CẢ dịch vụ AWS qua mọi region; đăng ký RSS để nhận alert gián đoạn
AWS Personal Health Dashboard = cá nhân hóa, chỉ hiển thị dịch vụ bạn đang dùng trong account và các sự kiện bảo trì ảnh hưởng đến bạn
AWS Health API cung cấp truy cập lập trình (JSON/HTTPS) vào dữ liệu Personal Health Dashboard; yêu cầu gói Business hoặc Enterprise

quiz.question: A company wants to receive an automated notification whenever the CPU utilization on a fleet of EC2 instances exceeds 80% for more than 5 minutes. Which CloudWatch feature should the solutions architect configure to meet this requirement with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: Create a CloudWatch Dashboard that displays CPU utilization and have an operator check it every hour
Create a CloudWatch Alarm that monitors the CPUUtilization metric and sends a notification to an SNS topic when the threshold is breached
Enable CloudWatch Logs on the instances and write a Lambda function to parse log entries for CPU spikes
Create a CloudWatch Events rule that polls EC2 instance metadata every 5 minutes and triggers an SNS notification
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Alarms are designed exactly for threshold-based notifications — they monitor a metric and send an alert via SNS when the threshold is breached. Dashboards require manual inspection. Using Logs and Lambda to parse CPU data is far more complex and unnecessary. CloudWatch Events is for event-driven triggers, not metric threshold monitoring.

quiz.question: A DevOps team is troubleshooting a production issue and needs to detect anomalies in application behavior with the finest possible time resolution. EC2 instances are currently using default CloudWatch monitoring. What change should the solutions architect recommend?
options: Switch from CloudWatch standard metrics to custom metrics, which always provide 1-second granularity
Enable detailed monitoring on the EC2 instances to reduce the metric interval from 5 minutes to 1 minute
Install the CloudWatch Logs agent to capture system-level metrics at sub-minute intervals
Create a CloudWatch Dashboard to aggregate metrics and set the refresh interval to 1 minute
quiz.explanation: By default, EC2 instances send metrics to CloudWatch every 5 minutes. Enabling detailed monitoring reduces this interval to 1 minute, providing finer granularity for troubleshooting. Custom metrics can support higher resolution but are not the default EC2 metric path. Dashboards control display, not data collection intervals.

quiz.question: An operations team receives a report that AWS is experiencing degraded performance in the us-east-1 region. A junior engineer wants to check whether specific AWS services used by their account are affected before escalating internally. Which tool provides the MOST targeted information for their account's resources?
options: AWS Service Health Dashboard, which shows the health of all AWS services globally
AWS Personal Health Dashboard, which shows events and maintenance affecting services used in their account
Amazon CloudWatch Alarms, which will alert them if any of their AWS resources become unhealthy
AWS Trusted Advisor, which continuously monitors account resources for performance and availability issues
quiz.explanation: The AWS Personal Health Dashboard is personalized to the account and shows only the services the account is using, including maintenance events and degradations affecting the account's specific resources. The Service Health Dashboard is public and covers all services/regions without account-level filtering. CloudWatch Alarms and Trusted Advisor serve different purposes.

quiz.question: A company's infrastructure team wants to build an internal dashboard that automatically displays the AWS service interruption status and proactively alerts on-call engineers via PagerDuty whenever a new AWS health event impacts their account. Which approach meets these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: Periodically scrape the AWS Service Health Dashboard RSS feed and parse HTML to extract relevant events
Use the AWS Health API to programmatically retrieve Personal Health Dashboard events and integrate with PagerDuty
Set up Amazon CloudWatch Logs Insights to query CloudTrail logs for service error events
Configure AWS Config rules to detect service interruptions and trigger SNS notifications
quiz.explanation: The AWS Health API provides programmatic JSON/HTTPS access to Personal Health Dashboard data, making it ideal for integration with external tools like PagerDuty. Scraping the public RSS feed only gives generic service status, not account-specific events. CloudWatch Logs Insights and AWS Config serve different purposes and would not capture AWS-side service health events.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is designing an observability strategy for a multi-tier web application on AWS. The team needs to collect application metrics, store and query application logs, and receive alerts when error rates exceed a threshold. Which TWO CloudWatch features should be used to meet these requirements? (Choose TWO)
options: CloudWatch Metrics to collect and store numerical time-series data from the application
CloudWatch Dashboards to automatically alert on-call engineers when thresholds are exceeded
CloudWatch Logs to store, query, and analyze application log data
CloudWatch Events to schedule periodic health checks against application endpoints
AWS Service Health Dashboard to monitor application error rates in real time
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Metrics is the correct feature for collecting time-series numerical data, and CloudWatch Logs is used to store and query log data from applications. Dashboards are for visualization, not alerting. CloudWatch Events handles event-driven rules and scheduling, not threshold alerting. The Service Health Dashboard is for AWS infrastructure status, not application metrics.

quiz.question: A company's AWS account is on the Developer support plan. A solutions architect is asked to automate the retrieval of AWS health events that impact their production workloads so they can integrate the data into their internal ITSM tool. What is a limiting factor they must address?
options: The AWS Health API is only available in the us-east-1 region
The AWS Health API requires a Business or Enterprise support plan to access
The AWS Personal Health Dashboard only retains health events for 24 hours
The AWS Health API does not support HTTPS and requires a VPN connection to access
quiz.explanation: Access to the AWS Health API requires at minimum a Business support plan. The Developer plan does not include API access to Personal Health Dashboard data. The Health API is globally accessible over HTTPS, not region-restricted or VPN-dependent. Health events are retained for 90 days, not just 24 hours.

quiz.question: During an AWS incident, a company wants all engineers to receive an email immediately when AWS announces a service degradation that could affect their account's running workloads. The solution must require MINIMAL setup and no custom code. What should the solutions architect recommend?
options: Subscribe to the AWS Service Health Dashboard RSS feed from each engineer's email client
Create a CloudWatch Alarm on the AWS/Health namespace that triggers an SNS email notification
Use the AWS Personal Health Dashboard to configure email alerts for health events affecting the account
Write a Lambda function that polls the AWS Health API every minute and sends emails via SES
quiz.explanation: The AWS Personal Health Dashboard supports configuring alerts (via SNS/email) for health events that affect your specific account, requiring minimal setup and no custom code. The public RSS feed covers all services/regions and is not account-specific. There is no AWS/Health CloudWatch namespace. Writing a Lambda polling solution works but involves significant operational overhead.

quiz.question: A solutions architect needs to build a single operational view that displays real-time metrics from EC2 instances in us-east-1 and eu-west-1 simultaneously for the NOC team. Which CloudWatch capability supports this requirement?
options: CloudWatch Alarms, which can aggregate metrics across regions into a single threshold
CloudWatch Logs Insights, which can run cross-region queries in a single console
CloudWatch Dashboards, which support adding widgets from multiple AWS regions into one view
CloudWatch Events, which can relay metrics from one region to another for centralized viewing
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Dashboards support cross-region views, allowing the team to add metric widgets from any AWS region into a single dashboard. CloudWatch Alarms evaluate metrics within a single region. Logs Insights queries are region-scoped. CloudWatch Events is an event routing service, not a metrics aggregation tool.

quiz.question: A company runs an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer. The team wants to automatically add EC2 instances when average CPU utilization across the group exceeds 70% for two consecutive periods. What is the MOST appropriate way to configure this?
options: Create a CloudWatch Event rule that triggers a Lambda function to call the Auto Scaling API when CPU exceeds 70%
Configure a CloudWatch Alarm on the CPUUtilization metric to trigger an Auto Scaling scale-out policy
Enable detailed monitoring and create a CloudWatch Dashboard alert to notify the Auto Scaling group
Write a CloudWatch Logs Insights scheduled query that detects high CPU and calls the Auto Scaling API
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Alarms can directly trigger Auto Scaling policies, which is the native and recommended pattern for metric-based scaling. Using Lambda via Events adds unnecessary complexity. Dashboards do not trigger actions. Logs Insights queries scheduled results cannot natively invoke scaling actions.

quiz.question: A company needs to understand the differences between the AWS Service Health Dashboard and the AWS Personal Health Dashboard before subscribing to alerts. Which TWO statements correctly describe these services? (Choose TWO)
options: The AWS Service Health Dashboard shows the health of all AWS services across all regions, visible to anyone publicly
The AWS Personal Health Dashboard requires an Enterprise support plan to access in the console
The AWS Personal Health Dashboard shows only health events and maintenance windows relevant to services in your account
The AWS Service Health Dashboard is personalized and filters events based on the services deployed in your VPC
Both dashboards provide identical information but differ only in their user interface layout
quiz.explanation: The AWS Service Health Dashboard is a public page showing the health of all AWS services globally — no login required. The Personal Health Dashboard is account-specific, showing only events affecting services you actually use. The Personal Health Dashboard is available to all accounts (not just Enterprise support), though API access requires Business/Enterprise. The Service Health Dashboard is not personalized.

quiz.question: An application team needs to trigger a serverless data processing pipeline every night at 11 PM UTC to generate daily reports. The pipeline entry point is a Lambda function. What is the MOST cost-effective solution with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Create a CloudWatch Alarm that evaluates a custom metric at 11 PM and triggers the Lambda function
Configure a CloudWatch Events rule with a cron schedule expression to invoke the Lambda function nightly
Deploy an EC2 instance with a cron job that calls the Lambda API at 11 PM each night
Use CloudWatch Logs to detect a daily log entry pattern at 11 PM and trigger the Lambda function
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Events (Amazon EventBridge) supports scheduled rules using cron or rate expressions, making it the native AWS mechanism to trigger Lambda on a schedule with zero infrastructure to manage. A CloudWatch Alarm requires a metric breach, not time-based scheduling. Running an EC2 instance solely for cron is costly and operationally heavy. Logs cannot trigger time-based invocations.

quiz.question: A security team wants to be notified within minutes when any IAM policy change is made in their AWS account, such as attaching a policy to a role. Which service combination achieves this with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: CloudWatch Logs Insights: run a scheduled query every minute against CloudTrail logs and send results via SES
CloudWatch Events rule that matches IAM policy change API calls from CloudTrail and routes to an SNS topic
AWS Config rule that evaluates IAM policy changes and sends a notification after a 15-minute evaluation window
CloudWatch Alarm on the IAM/PolicyChanges custom metric namespace that fires when the value is greater than 0
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Events integrates with CloudTrail to detect API calls in near real-time and can route matching events (e.g., AttachRolePolicy) to SNS for immediate notification. Logs Insights scheduled queries introduce delay and added complexity. AWS Config evaluations are not real-time. IAM does not natively publish a PolicyChanges metric to CloudWatch.

quiz.question: A company monitors 50 EC2 instances and wants to identify performance trends over the past 30 days using metrics like CPU, network, and disk I/O. The NOC manager wants this information in a single view accessible to the entire team. What is the MOST appropriate CloudWatch feature to use?
options: CloudWatch Alarms, configured with a 30-day evaluation period to surface trend violations
CloudWatch Logs Insights, used to run queries against the last 30 days of metric data
CloudWatch Dashboards, configured with time-series widgets displaying metrics over the desired period
CloudWatch Events, set to capture and store hourly metric snapshots into an S3 bucket for team review
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Dashboards are designed for visualizing metrics over custom time windows and can be shared with the team. Alarms evaluate metrics against thresholds but do not visualize trends. Logs Insights queries logs, not raw CloudWatch metrics. CloudWatch Events is an event routing service, not a metrics visualization tool.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is evaluating how to respond to EC2 instance failures automatically. The team wants CloudWatch to detect the failure and trigger two actions: replace the instance via Auto Scaling AND send an SMS notification to the on-call engineer. Which THREE components are needed to implement this solution? (Choose THREE)
options: A CloudWatch Alarm monitoring the EC2 StatusCheckFailed metric
An SNS topic with an SMS subscription for the on-call engineer
An Auto Scaling policy configured as an alarm action on the CloudWatch Alarm
A CloudWatch Events rule that polls EC2 health checks every 30 seconds
A CloudWatch Dashboard widget to display the alarm state to the team
quiz.explanation: The solution needs: (1) a CloudWatch Alarm on StatusCheckFailed to detect the failure, (2) an Auto Scaling policy as an alarm action to replace the instance, and (3) an SNS topic with an SMS subscription for engineer notification — the alarm can trigger both actions simultaneously. CloudWatch Events polling is redundant when an Alarm already monitors the metric. A Dashboard widget adds visibility but does not contribute to automated response.

quiz.question: A startup has EC2 instances with default CloudWatch monitoring enabled. A developer notices that during a recent 3-minute traffic spike, the CloudWatch CPU metric did not capture the peak. The developer wants to ensure future short-lived spikes are recorded. What is the MOST straightforward fix?
options: Install the CloudWatch agent and publish a custom metric with 10-second resolution
Enable detailed monitoring on the EC2 instances to change the reporting interval from 5 minutes to 1 minute
Increase the CloudWatch Alarm evaluation period to 10 minutes to smooth out short spikes
Create a CloudWatch Dashboard with a 1-minute refresh rate to poll metrics more frequently
quiz.explanation: Default EC2 monitoring sends metrics every 5 minutes, which means a 3-minute spike can be missed entirely. Enabling detailed monitoring changes the interval to 1 minute, capturing short-lived spikes. A custom metric with higher resolution is possible but requires agent installation and custom code — more overhead than enabling detailed monitoring. Increasing alarm period would make detection worse, not better. Dashboard refresh rate does not affect data collection frequency.

quiz.question: An enterprise customer has a Business support plan and wants to automate incident management by pulling AWS health event data into their ServiceNow instance whenever a health event is created or updated for services in their account. What is the BEST approach?
options: Poll the AWS Service Health Dashboard HTML page every minute and parse new events into ServiceNow
Set up a CloudWatch Alarm on the AWS/Health namespace and forward alarm notifications to ServiceNow
Use the AWS Health API to retrieve Personal Health Dashboard events programmatically and push them to ServiceNow
Subscribe to the AWS Service Health Dashboard RSS feed and configure an RSS-to-webhook bridge into ServiceNow
quiz.explanation: The AWS Health API provides structured JSON access to Personal Health Dashboard events, which is ideal for integration with ITSM tools like ServiceNow. With a Business support plan, the account has API access. Scraping HTML or using the public RSS feed provides generic, non-account-specific data. There is no AWS/Health CloudWatch namespace.

quiz.question: A media company stores application logs in CloudWatch Logs. The security team wants to run ad-hoc queries to find all occurrences of a specific error code across millions of log entries from the past 7 days. Which CloudWatch capability is MOST appropriate for this use case?
options: CloudWatch Alarms, by creating a metric filter that counts occurrences of the error code
CloudWatch Dashboards, by adding a log widget that displays filtered log streams
CloudWatch Logs Insights, by running an interactive query against the log group over the 7-day window
CloudWatch Events, by creating a pattern-matching rule that captures the error code in real time
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch Logs Insights is the purpose-built feature for interactively querying log data at scale, supporting custom query syntax over arbitrary time ranges. Metric filters count occurrences going forward but cannot retroactively query historical data. Dashboards display data but do not run ad-hoc queries. CloudWatch Events captures events in near real-time going forward, not historical log data.

quiz.question: A company wants to be notified via email when AWS schedules a maintenance event that could cause a brief reboot of their RDS instances, and also when any EC2 instance in their account is scheduled for retirement. Which TWO services should the solutions architect use to implement this with MINIMAL custom code? (Choose TWO)
options: AWS Personal Health Dashboard to surface account-specific maintenance and retirement events
Amazon CloudWatch Alarms to detect RDS reboot metrics and trigger SNS email notifications
Amazon SNS to deliver email notifications when health events are raised
AWS Service Health Dashboard to monitor scheduled maintenance for all RDS and EC2 services globally
AWS CloudTrail to log and alert on RDS maintenance and EC2 retirement API calls
quiz.explanation: The AWS Personal Health Dashboard surfaces account-specific events like scheduled maintenance and EC2 retirement notices, and these events can trigger SNS notifications for email delivery. The Service Health Dashboard is public and not account-specific — it would not filter to just the account's affected resources. CloudWatch Alarms monitor metrics, not scheduled maintenance windows. CloudTrail logs API calls but cannot detect future scheduled events.

lessons.title: AWS Audit and Compliance Services
lessons.overview: Learn the three AWS services used for auditing and compliance: CloudTrail (who did what and when), AWS Artifact (download compliance reports), and AWS Security Hub (centralized security posture across accounts). Key SAA exam topic for governance and regulatory compliance scenarios.
lessons.overviewVi: Tìm hiểu 3 dịch vụ AWS dùng cho audit và tuân thủ: CloudTrail (ai làm gì và khi nào), AWS Artifact (tải báo cáo tuân thủ), và AWS Security Hub (tổng quan bảo mật tập trung qua nhiều account). Chủ đề quan trọng trong SAA về quản trị và tuân thủ quy định.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Visibility Into Who Did What
sections.contentHtml:
Audit and compliance services help you track API operations and user actions across your AWS infrastructure — answering questions like: who created this resource? When was this permission changed? Which API calls were made?

AWS CloudTrailTracks user activity and API usage across your AWS account
AWS ArtifactOn-demand portal for downloading AWS compliance reports (ISO, PCI, SOC)
AWS Security HubCentralized security posture view aggregating findings from multiple AWS security services
sections.contentVi: Các dịch vụ audit và compliance giúp theo dõi API operations và hành động người dùng trên hạ tầng AWS — trả lời câu hỏi: ai đã tạo resource này? Khi nào quyền này bị thay đổi? API call nào được thực hiện? AWS CloudTrail: theo dõi hoạt động người dùng và API usage. AWS Artifact: portal tải báo cáo tuân thủ AWS theo yêu cầu (ISO, PCI, SOC). AWS Security Hub: tổng quan bảo mật tập trung, tổng hợp findings từ nhiều dịch vụ bảo mật AWS.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: AWS CloudTrail: Activity Auditing
sections.contentHtml:
AWS CloudTrail is an IT audit service that tracks user activity and API usage in your AWS account. It continuously monitors and logs account activities from the AWS Management Console, SDKs, APIs, and CLI. Logs are stored in an Amazon S3 bucket.

CloudTrail records two types of events:

Management Events (Control Plane)Operations on resources — e.g., attaching an IAM role, creating a VPC, creating a subnet
Data Events (Data Plane)Operations on or within a resource — e.g., Amazon S3 object-level API activities, invoking a Lambda function
Cross-account logging: You can receive CloudTrail logs from multiple AWS accounts by granting cross-account permissions — each account delivers logs to an S3 bucket in a central account.
sections.contentVi: AWS CloudTrail là dịch vụ IT audit theo dõi hoạt động người dùng và API usage trong AWS account. Liên tục monitor và ghi log hoạt động từ AWS Management Console, SDKs, APIs và CLI. Log lưu trong Amazon S3 bucket. CloudTrail ghi lại 2 loại sự kiện: Management Events (Control Plane) — thao tác trên resource, ví dụ: gắn IAM role, tạo VPC, tạo subnet. Data Events (Data Plane) — thao tác trên hoặc trong resource, ví dụ: S3 object-level API, gọi Lambda function. Cross-account logging: bạn có thể nhận CloudTrail logs từ nhiều AWS account bằng cách cấp cross-account permissions — mỗi account gửi log đến S3 bucket trong account trung tâm.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: AWS Artifact and AWS Security Hub
sections.contentHtml:
AWS Artifact is a self-service portal providing on-demand AWS security and compliance reports.

  • ISO reports
  • Payment Card Industry (PCI) reports
  • Service Organization Control (SOC) reports
  • Certifications from accreditation bodies globally

Use Artifact to download compliance documents like SOC 1 reports and ISO certifications to share with auditors.

AWS Security Hub provides a centralized, comprehensive view of the security posture of your cloud infrastructure across multiple AWS accounts.

  • Works like a hub: aggregates security alerts and findings from Amazon GuardDuty, Amazon Inspector, Amazon Macie, IAM Access Analyzer, AWS Firewall Manager, and other sources
  • Helps comply with: PCI DSS (Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard), CIS (Center for Internet Security) benchmarks, and other security standards
sections.contentVi: AWS Artifact là portal tự phục vụ cung cấp báo cáo bảo mật và tuân thủ AWS theo yêu cầu: ISO reports, PCI (Payment Card Industry) reports, SOC (Service Organization Control) reports, chứng chỉ từ các tổ chức kiểm định toàn cầu. Dùng Artifact để tải tài liệu tuân thủ như SOC 1 reports và ISO certifications cho kiểm toán viên. AWS Security Hub cung cấp tổng quan bảo mật tập trung và toàn diện cho hạ tầng cloud qua nhiều AWS account. Hoạt động như hub: tổng hợp security alerts và findings từ Amazon GuardDuty, Amazon Inspector, Amazon Macie, IAM Access Analyzer, AWS Firewall Manager và các nguồn khác. Hỗ trợ tuân thủ: PCI DSS, CIS benchmarks và các tiêu chuẩn bảo mật khác.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Audit Services: Key Exam Points
sections.contentHtml:
• CloudTrail = who did what and when; logs to S3; covers Console, SDK, API, CLI actions
  • Management Events = control plane (IAM, VPC, subnet changes); Data Events = data plane (S3 object access, Lambda invocations)
  • CloudTrail ≠ CloudWatch: CloudTrail = audit API/user activity; CloudWatch = monitor metrics and system performance
  • AWS Artifact = compliance report downloads (ISO, PCI, SOC); self-service, on-demand
  • Security Hub = aggregate security findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, IAM Access Analyzer, Firewall Manager in one place
Exam trap: If a question asks "who made this API call" or "track user activity" → CloudTrail. If it asks "monitor CPU/disk/memory performance" → CloudWatch. If it asks "download SOC or ISO compliance report" → AWS Artifact.
sections.contentVi: Điểm thi quan trọng: CloudTrail = ai làm gì và khi nào; ghi log vào S3; bao gồm Console, SDK, API, CLI. Management Events = control plane (IAM, VPC, subnet); Data Events = data plane (S3 object access, Lambda invocations). CloudTrail ≠ CloudWatch: CloudTrail = audit API/user activity; CloudWatch = monitor metrics và hiệu suất hệ thống. AWS Artifact = tải báo cáo tuân thủ (ISO, PCI, SOC); tự phục vụ, theo yêu cầu. Security Hub = tổng hợp findings bảo mật từ GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, IAM Access Analyzer, Firewall Manager tại một nơi. Bẫy thi: 'ai thực hiện API call' hoặc 'theo dõi hoạt động user' → CloudTrail; 'monitor CPU/disk/memory' → CloudWatch; 'tải báo cáo SOC hoặc ISO' → AWS Artifact.

summary: AWS CloudTrail tracks all user activity and API calls in your account — Management Events (control plane: IAM/VPC changes) and Data Events (data plane: S3 object access, Lambda invocations); logs stored in S3
CloudTrail supports cross-account logging: multiple accounts can deliver logs to one central S3 bucket
AWS Artifact is a self-service portal for downloading AWS compliance reports on demand: ISO, PCI, SOC certifications
AWS Security Hub aggregates security findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, IAM Access Analyzer, and Firewall Manager into a centralized security posture view across multiple accounts
Key exam distinction: CloudTrail = audit who/what/when; CloudWatch = monitor performance metrics; Artifact = compliance report downloads
summaryVi: AWS CloudTrail theo dõi toàn bộ hoạt động user và API calls — Management Events (control plane: IAM/VPC) và Data Events (data plane: S3 object, Lambda); log lưu trong S3
CloudTrail hỗ trợ cross-account logging: nhiều account gửi log về một S3 bucket trung tâm
AWS Artifact là portal tự phục vụ để tải báo cáo tuân thủ AWS theo yêu cầu: ISO, PCI, SOC certifications
AWS Security Hub tổng hợp findings bảo mật từ GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, IAM Access Analyzer và Firewall Manager vào một tổng quan bảo mật tập trung qua nhiều account
Phân biệt thi quan trọng: CloudTrail = audit ai/gì/khi nào; CloudWatch = monitor metrics hiệu suất; Artifact = tải báo cáo tuân thủ

quiz.question: A security team needs to investigate which IAM user deleted an S3 bucket in their AWS account last week. Which service should they use to find this information?
options: Amazon CloudWatch Logs — search for S3 bucket deletion events in the log groups
AWS CloudTrail — review the event history for the DeleteBucket API call
AWS Config — query the configuration timeline for the missing bucket
AWS Security Hub — check the findings dashboard for unauthorized deletions
quiz.explanation: CloudTrail records every API call including who made it, when, and from where — making it the correct tool for auditing the DeleteBucket event. CloudWatch monitors performance metrics, not API call history. AWS Config tracks resource configuration changes but does not capture the identity performing the action as directly as CloudTrail. Security Hub aggregates findings but does not store raw API call logs.

quiz.question: A company has 20 AWS accounts managed under AWS Organizations. The compliance team wants a single, tamper-resistant archive of all API activity across every account with the LEAST operational overhead. What is the BEST approach?
options: Enable CloudTrail in each account and configure each trail to send logs to its own S3 bucket; aggregate using Amazon Athena
Create an organization-level CloudTrail trail that delivers logs from all accounts to one central S3 bucket
Deploy a Lambda function in each account to forward CloudWatch Logs to a central account
Use AWS Security Hub to collect and store API activity logs across all accounts
quiz.explanation: An organization-level CloudTrail trail automatically applies to all current and future member accounts and delivers all logs to a single S3 bucket — requiring no per-account setup and providing the least operational overhead. Maintaining separate trails per account increases management burden. Lambda-based forwarding is complex and error-prone. Security Hub aggregates security findings, not raw CloudTrail logs.

quiz.question: A healthcare company is preparing for an external audit and must provide evidence that AWS complies with HIPAA, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 standards. What is the MOST cost-effective way to obtain these compliance reports?
options: Contact AWS Support and request the compliance documents through a support ticket
Hire a third-party auditor to assess the AWS infrastructure and generate the reports
Download the reports on demand from AWS Artifact at no additional cost
Use AWS Security Hub to generate compliance reports against the relevant frameworks
quiz.explanation: AWS Artifact is a self-service portal that provides on-demand access to AWS compliance reports such as ISO, SOC, and PCI certifications at no additional cost. Opening support tickets or hiring auditors introduces unnecessary cost and delay. Security Hub assesses your workload's security posture but does not provide AWS's own third-party audit certifications.

quiz.question: An architect is setting up logging for a financial application. The team needs to track every time an object is read from or written to a specific S3 bucket for compliance reasons. Which CloudTrail event type must be enabled?
options: Management Events, because S3 operations are part of the AWS control plane
Data Events, because S3 object-level operations are data plane activities
Insight Events, because unusual S3 access patterns indicate potential threats
Network Activity Events, because S3 transfers occur over the network layer
quiz.explanation: Data Events capture resource-level operations such as S3 GetObject, PutObject, and DeleteObject — these are data plane actions. Management Events capture control plane activities like creating or deleting a bucket (IAM, VPC, S3 bucket-level changes). Insight Events detect anomalous API call rates but do not log individual object operations. Network Activity Events are not a standard CloudTrail category.

quiz.question: A company's operations team wants to be alerted when CPU utilization on their EC2 fleet exceeds 80%. A security analyst separately wants to know when someone disables CloudTrail logging. Which services should each team use, respectively?
options: CloudTrail for both teams — it captures all AWS activity including performance metrics
CloudWatch for the operations team; CloudTrail for the security analyst
CloudTrail for the operations team; CloudWatch for the security analyst
AWS Security Hub for both teams — it consolidates all monitoring and audit data
quiz.explanation: CloudWatch monitors performance metrics like CPU utilization and can trigger alarms. CloudTrail records API calls such as StopLogging (disabling CloudTrail), making it the right tool for the security analyst. These two services serve distinct purposes and are not interchangeable — CloudTrail does not collect metric data, and CloudWatch does not log API call history.

quiz.question: A company wants a centralized view of its security posture across 15 AWS accounts, aggregating findings from multiple AWS security services. Which TWO services produce findings that AWS Security Hub can ingest? (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon GuardDuty
AWS CloudTrail
Amazon Macie
AWS Artifact
Amazon CloudWatch
quiz.explanation: Security Hub ingests findings from GuardDuty (threat detection), Macie (sensitive data discovery), Inspector, IAM Access Analyzer, and Firewall Manager. CloudTrail stores API audit logs but does not produce security findings that Security Hub aggregates. Artifact is a compliance document portal, and CloudWatch is a performance monitoring service — neither feeds findings into Security Hub.

quiz.question: A startup is building a multi-tenant SaaS platform across three AWS accounts: dev, staging, and production. A compliance officer asks for a solution to ensure all API calls in all three accounts are logged to a single, immutable location. What should the solutions architect recommend?
options: Enable CloudWatch detailed monitoring in all three accounts and stream logs to a central Kinesis Data Firehose
Enable CloudTrail in each account individually and use S3 Cross-Region Replication to merge the logs
Create a CloudTrail trail in the management account that applies to all accounts in the AWS Organization and delivers logs to a central S3 bucket with S3 Object Lock enabled
Deploy AWS Config rules across all accounts and use an aggregator to collect compliance data centrally
quiz.explanation: An organization-level CloudTrail trail covers all member accounts automatically; delivering to a central S3 bucket with S3 Object Lock (WORM) ensures immutability. Managing separate trails per account increases overhead and risks gaps. CloudWatch does not capture API call history. AWS Config tracks configuration compliance, not API call auditing.

quiz.question: A developer asks whether enabling CloudTrail Data Events for all S3 buckets will have cost implications compared to only enabling Management Events. Which statement is correct?
options: Data Events and Management Events are both included in the free tier and have no additional cost
Management Events are charged additionally while Data Events are always free
Data Events incur additional charges because they log high-volume data plane operations, whereas the first copy of Management Events is free per trail
Both event types cost the same fixed monthly fee regardless of volume
quiz.explanation: CloudTrail provides the first copy of Management Events for free per trail. Data Events (S3 object-level, Lambda invocations) are charged based on volume because they can generate extremely high event counts in busy environments. Understanding this distinction helps architects make cost-conscious decisions about what to log.

quiz.question: An enterprise CISO wants a single dashboard showing security alerts from Amazon Inspector, IAM Access Analyzer, and Amazon GuardDuty across all AWS accounts in the organization. Which service provides this capability with the LEAST operational overhead?
options: AWS CloudTrail Lake — query all security events using SQL across accounts
AWS Security Hub with Organizations integration — automatically aggregate findings from all accounts into a delegated administrator account
Amazon CloudWatch dashboards — create custom widgets that pull metrics from each security service
AWS Artifact — subscribe to security finding reports for each account
quiz.explanation: Security Hub with AWS Organizations integration automatically collects findings from Inspector, IAM Access Analyzer, GuardDuty, and others into a single delegated administrator account with minimal setup. CloudTrail Lake stores and queries API logs, not security findings. CloudWatch dashboards require custom metric definitions and do not natively aggregate security findings. Artifact is for compliance report downloads, not real-time findings.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is designing an audit strategy for a regulated financial institution. The institution requires proof of both AWS's own compliance certifications AND visibility into who accessed sensitive customer data stored in S3. Which TWO services should the architect include in the solution? (Choose TWO)
options: AWS Artifact — to download AWS SOC 2 and PCI DSS reports
AWS Security Hub — to consolidate security findings into a central dashboard
AWS CloudTrail with Data Events enabled on the S3 bucket — to log every object-level access
Amazon CloudWatch — to monitor S3 request rates and latency
AWS Config — to record changes to the S3 bucket's configuration
quiz.explanation: AWS Artifact provides AWS's own compliance certifications (SOC, PCI, ISO) needed for audit evidence. CloudTrail with Data Events captures exactly who accessed each S3 object and when, satisfying the data access audit requirement. Security Hub aggregates findings but does not provide AWS compliance certificates. CloudWatch monitors performance metrics, not access identity. AWS Config tracks configuration history, not data access.

quiz.question: A security engineer notices that CloudTrail is enabled but the CloudTrail log files in S3 have not been updated for 48 hours. What is the MOST likely explanation and the best first step to investigate?
options: Check CloudWatch metrics for the S3 bucket to see if the bucket is throttling put requests
Review CloudTrail event history in the console for a StopLogging API call to determine if someone disabled the trail
Open an AWS Support ticket because CloudTrail outages must be reported by AWS
Delete and recreate the CloudTrail trail to restore logging immediately without further investigation
quiz.explanation: If CloudTrail stops delivering logs, a common cause is that someone called the StopLogging API — which itself would be recorded in the last available event history. Reviewing CloudTrail event history for StopLogging is the fastest diagnostic step. CloudWatch S3 metrics would not explain a deliberate logging disable. Recreating the trail without investigating would miss a potential security incident.

quiz.question: A company's legal team needs to sign an AWS Business Associate Agreement (BAA) for HIPAA compliance and also wants to download the AWS ISO 27001 certification. Where should they go to accomplish both tasks?
options: AWS Security Hub — compliance agreements and certifications are managed through the security console
AWS Artifact — agreements (like BAA) can be reviewed and accepted, and compliance reports can be downloaded, all from one portal
AWS Config — the compliance dashboard includes regulatory agreements and audit certificates
AWS Trusted Advisor — the compliance section lists available certifications and agreements
quiz.explanation: AWS Artifact serves two purposes: (1) it allows customers to download AWS compliance reports such as ISO, SOC, and PCI certifications, and (2) it provides a section called Artifact Agreements where customers can review and accept agreements like the HIPAA BAA. Security Hub, Config, and Trusted Advisor do not host compliance agreements or certification documents.

quiz.question: An auditor asks a solutions architect to explain what AWS CloudTrail Management Events capture. Which of the following is an example of a Management Event?
options: A Lambda function being invoked with a specific payload
A user downloading an object from an S3 bucket via GetObject
An administrator creating a new VPC using the AWS console
An application writing a record to a DynamoDB table
quiz.explanation: Management Events (control plane operations) include actions that configure or manage AWS resources — such as CreateVpc, CreateBucket, AttachRolePolicy, and RunInstances. Lambda invocations, S3 GetObject, and DynamoDB PutItem are data plane operations captured by Data Events, not Management Events.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is asked to differentiate between CloudTrail and CloudWatch to a new team member. Which THREE statements correctly distinguish the two services? (Choose THREE)
options: CloudTrail records API calls and user activity; CloudWatch monitors resource performance metrics and logs
CloudTrail can trigger alarms when CPU utilization exceeds a threshold; CloudWatch cannot
CloudWatch can send alerts when an unusual number of API calls is detected via CloudTrail integration
CloudTrail logs are stored in S3; CloudWatch metrics and logs are stored in CloudWatch
CloudTrail is primarily used to answer 'who did what and when'; CloudWatch is used to answer 'how is my system performing'
quiz.explanation: CloudTrail audits API/user activity and stores logs in S3; CloudWatch monitors operational performance and stores metrics/logs in its own service. The distinction 'who/what/when vs. how is performance' is the key exam differentiator. CloudTrail does not trigger CPU alarms — that is CloudWatch's role. While CloudWatch alarms can be set on CloudTrail metrics, it is CloudWatch doing the alerting, not CloudTrail itself.

quiz.question: A startup with a single AWS account enables CloudTrail for the first time. By default, what does CloudTrail begin logging immediately after being enabled, without any additional configuration?
options: Both Management Events and Data Events for all services in the region
Management Events only — Data Events must be explicitly enabled and may incur extra charges
Data Events only for S3 and Lambda, because they are the most commonly audited services
No events — a trail must first be manually created and an S3 bucket destination must be specified before any logging begins
quiz.explanation: By default, CloudTrail logs Management Events (control plane API calls) and provides 90 days of event history in the console at no charge. Data Events (S3 object-level access, Lambda invocations) are not enabled by default and require explicit configuration, incurring additional cost due to their high volume. A trail is needed to persist logs beyond 90 days to S3, but the event history view is available immediately.

quiz.question: A security team at a multinational corporation receives daily alerts from GuardDuty, weekly reports from Amazon Inspector, and ad-hoc findings from IAM Access Analyzer — all across 30 AWS accounts. Analysts spend hours manually correlating these findings. Which solution would MOST reduce this operational burden?
options: Enable CloudTrail across all accounts and use CloudTrail Lake to query findings with SQL
Create a custom Lambda function that polls each service's API every hour and writes results to a central DynamoDB table
Enable AWS Security Hub with AWS Organizations integration and designate a delegated administrator account to aggregate all findings
Configure Amazon CloudWatch composite alarms that reference metrics from each security service across all accounts
quiz.explanation: Security Hub with Organizations integration automatically aggregates findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, IAM Access Analyzer, Macie, and Firewall Manager across all accounts into a single pane of glass with no custom code required. CloudTrail Lake handles audit log queries, not security finding aggregation. A custom Lambda solution would require significant development and maintenance overhead. CloudWatch composite alarms address metric-based alerting, not cross-service finding correlation.

quiz.question: During a security review, an auditor asks to verify that no IAM policies were modified in the past 30 days without authorization. What is the MOST direct way to provide this evidence?
options: Run an AWS Trusted Advisor check for IAM policy changes and export the results
Search CloudTrail event history for IAM API calls such as CreatePolicy, AttachRolePolicy, and PutUserPolicy
Review Amazon Macie findings for any detected IAM policy documents in S3
Check AWS Security Hub for findings categorized under the IAM Controls compliance standard
quiz.explanation: CloudTrail records all IAM control plane operations as Management Events, including CreatePolicy, DeletePolicy, AttachRolePolicy, and PutUserPolicy — providing a complete audit trail of who made what change and when. Trusted Advisor checks current configurations, not historical change logs. Macie detects sensitive data in S3, not IAM changes. Security Hub may surface IAM misconfigurations but does not log the specific API calls that made changes.

quiz.question: A company wants to ensure its AWS environment meets the requirements of an upcoming PCI DSS audit. The auditor requests both evidence of AWS's own PCI certification AND a log showing all administrative actions taken in the AWS account over the past year. Which TWO actions should the solutions architect take? (Choose TWO)
options: Download the AWS PCI DSS Attestation of Compliance from AWS Artifact
Enable AWS Security Hub and run the PCI DSS security standard check
Configure a CloudTrail trail to deliver Management Events to an S3 bucket with a 1-year retention policy
Use Amazon CloudWatch to create a dashboard showing all administrative API call rates
Use AWS Config to generate a compliance report for all PCI-related resource configurations
quiz.explanation: AWS Artifact provides AWS's own PCI DSS Attestation of Compliance, which auditors require as proof that the underlying infrastructure is PCI-certified. A CloudTrail trail with long-term S3 retention captures all Management Events (administrative actions) and satisfies the audit log requirement. Security Hub's PCI standard identifies misconfigurations but does not provide AWS's certification. CloudWatch dashboards show metrics, not identity-linked API call history. AWS Config tracks configuration state, not who performed administrative actions.

lessons.title: AWS Networking and Content Delivery Services
lessons.overview: Survey the full range of AWS networking services — from VPC as the private cloud foundation to load balancers, DNS routing, CDN, hybrid connectivity, and API management — and learn which service to choose in each scenario.
lessons.overviewVi: Khảo sát toàn bộ dịch vụ mạng AWS — từ VPC làm nền tảng mạng riêng tư, đến load balancer, định tuyến DNS, CDN, kết nối hybrid và quản lý API — và học cách chọn đúng dịch vụ cho từng tình huống.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Networking Services Landscape
sections.contentHtml:
AWS networking services enable secure communication between components and deliver content globally with low latency. They cover four main concerns:

Isolation & routingAmazon VPC, Route 53, AWS Transit Gateway
Traffic distributionElastic Load Balancing — ALB, NLB, GWLB, CLB
Content deliveryAmazon CloudFront (CDN), AWS Global Accelerator
Hybrid / private connectivityAWS VPN, AWS Direct Connect, AWS PrivateLink
API & service meshAmazon API Gateway, AWS App Mesh, AWS Cloud Map

Understanding which service solves which problem is one of the highest-value skills for the SAA-C03 exam.
sections.contentVi: Các dịch vụ mạng AWS kết nối tài nguyên bảo mật bên trong và giữa các mạng, đồng thời phân phối nội dung toàn cầu với độ trễ thấp. Chúng bao phủ 5 nhóm chính: Cô lập và định tuyến — Amazon VPC, Route 53, AWS Transit Gateway; Phân phối traffic — Elastic Load Balancing (ALB, NLB, GWLB, CLB); Phân phối nội dung — Amazon CloudFront (CDN), AWS Global Accelerator; Kết nối hybrid/riêng tư — AWS VPN, AWS Direct Connect, AWS PrivateLink; API và service mesh — Amazon API Gateway, AWS App Mesh, AWS Cloud Map. Hiểu rõ dịch vụ nào giải quyết vấn đề gì là kỹ năng có giá trị cao nhất cho kỳ thi SAA-C03.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon VPC: Private Cloud Networking
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) is a logically isolated private network in AWS. You have full control over IP address ranges, subnets, route tables, and gateways.

Internet GatewayProvides inbound/outbound internet connectivity to the VPC
Virtual Private GatewayConnects VPC to an on-premises network via VPN or Direct Connect
VPC PeeringPrivate connection between two VPCs — no transitive routing
VPC EndpointPrivate connection to AWS services (e.g. S3) without traversing the public internet
AWS OutpostsExtend VPC subnets to on-premises hardware racks
Note: A newly created VPC is completely private by default — no internet access until an Internet Gateway is attached and routes are configured.

Elastic Load Balancing (ELB) distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets for high availability. Choose the right type based on protocol:

ALB (Application)HTTP/HTTPS — web apps, microservices, containers; path-based and host-based routing (Layer 7)
NLB (Network)TCP/UDP/TLS — ultra-low latency, billions of requests/second, static IP (Layer 4)
GWLB (Gateway)Layer 3 (IP) — deploy and scale third-party virtual appliances: firewalls, IDS/IPS
CLB (Classic)Legacy — EC2-Classic networks, custom security policies, TCP passthrough
Exam tip: ALB = Layer 7 (HTTP/path routing). NLB = Layer 4 (TCP/UDP, static IP, extreme throughput). GWLB = Layer 3 (third-party security appliances).
sections.contentVi: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) là mạng riêng tư cô lập logic trong AWS. Bạn kiểm soát hoàn toàn dải IP, subnet, bảng định tuyến và gateway. Internet Gateway — kết nối internet vào/ra VPC; Virtual Private Gateway — kết nối VPC đến mạng on-premises qua VPN hoặc Direct Connect; VPC Peering — kết nối riêng tư giữa 2 VPC (không hỗ trợ định tuyến bắc cầu); VPC Endpoint — kết nối riêng tư đến dịch vụ AWS (như S3) mà không qua internet; AWS Outposts — mở rộng subnet VPC ra phần cứng on-premises. Lưu ý: VPC mới mặc định hoàn toàn riêng tư — không có internet cho đến khi gắn Internet Gateway và cấu hình route. Elastic Load Balancing phân phối traffic đến nhiều target. Phân loại: ALB — HTTP/HTTPS, định tuyến theo path/host (Layer 7); NLB — TCP/UDP/TLS, độ trễ cực thấp, hàng tỷ request/giây, IP tĩnh (Layer 4); GWLB — Layer 3 (IP), triển khai virtual appliances bên thứ ba (tường lửa, IDS/IPS); CLB — legacy, dùng cho EC2-Classic. Mẹo thi: ALB=Layer 7, NLB=Layer 4, GWLB=Layer 3.

sections.label: Deep Dive
sections.heading: Route 53, CloudFront, and Connectivity Options
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon Route 53 is a fully managed DNS service. It supports domain registration and multiple routing policies:

  • Failover — active/passive for disaster recovery
  • Latency — routes to the region with the lowest latency for the user
  • Geolocation — routes based on the user's geographic location
  • Geoproximity — routes by resource proximity with configurable bias
  • Weighted — splits traffic by percentage (e.g. 80/20 blue-green)
  • Multivalue Answer — returns multiple healthy IPs for basic load distribution
CloudFrontCDN — caches static content (images, video, downloads) at edge locations globally; origin can be S3, ALB, or custom HTTP
Global AcceleratorStatic Anycast IPs + AWS backbone routing — accelerates dynamic/uncacheable content; NO caching
Direct ConnectDedicated private line from on-premises to AWS — consistent bandwidth, does NOT traverse the public internet
AWS VPN (Site-to-Site)IPsec encrypted tunnel over the public internet — quick setup, lower cost than Direct Connect
Transit GatewayHub-and-spoke connecting hundreds of VPCs, VPNs, and Direct Connect Gateways in a single managed service
AWS PrivateLinkProvides private connectivity to AWS services and third-party services via VPC Endpoints — traffic stays on AWS network
CloudFront vs Global Accelerator: CloudFront caches content at edge (best for static assets). Global Accelerator does NOT cache — it accelerates the connection via the AWS global network (best for dynamic APIs, gaming, VoIP).
sections.contentVi: Amazon Route 53 là dịch vụ DNS fully managed hỗ trợ đăng ký domain và nhiều routing policy: Failover — active/passive cho disaster recovery; Latency — định tuyến đến region có độ trễ thấp nhất; Geolocation — định tuyến theo vị trí địa lý người dùng; Geoproximity — định tuyến theo khoảng cách đến tài nguyên (có bias); Weighted — chia traffic theo phần trăm (blue-green 80/20); Multivalue Answer — trả nhiều IP healthy để phân phối cơ bản. CloudFront — CDN, cache nội dung tĩnh (ảnh, video, downloads) tại edge locations toàn cầu; origin có thể là S3, ALB hoặc custom HTTP. Global Accelerator — IP Anycast tĩnh + định tuyến qua mạng backbone AWS, tăng tốc nội dung động/không cache được, KHÔNG cache. Direct Connect — đường riêng từ on-premises đến AWS, băng thông ổn định, KHÔNG qua internet công cộng. AWS VPN (Site-to-Site) — tunnel IPsec qua internet, cài đặt nhanh, rẻ hơn Direct Connect. Transit Gateway — hub kết nối hàng trăm VPC, VPN và Direct Connect Gateway trong một dịch vụ. AWS PrivateLink — kết nối riêng tư đến dịch vụ AWS qua VPC Endpoints, traffic không rời mạng AWS. Phân biệt CloudFront vs Global Accelerator: CloudFront cache nội dung (nội dung tĩnh). Global Accelerator KHÔNG cache — tăng tốc kết nối qua backbone AWS (nội dung động, gaming, VoIP).

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Keyword Map and Key Distinctions
sections.contentHtml:
• Dedicated private line (no public internet): AWS Direct Connect
  • Encrypted tunnel over public internet: AWS Site-to-Site VPN
  • Hub for many VPCs / transitive routing: AWS Transit Gateway (VPC Peering is non-transitive)
  • Cache static content globally: Amazon CloudFront (CDN)
  • Static IP single entry point / dynamic content acceleration: AWS Global Accelerator
  • HTTP path-based routing / microservices: Application Load Balancer (ALB)
  • Ultra-low latency TCP/UDP / billions req/s: Network Load Balancer (NLB)
  • Third-party firewalls / IDS/IPS: Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB)
  • DNS + domain registration: Amazon Route 53
  • Private access to S3/DynamoDB without internet: VPC Gateway Endpoint; other AWS services: VPC Interface Endpoint (PrivateLink)
  • RESTful API / WebSocket managed endpoint: Amazon API Gateway
Direct Connect vs VPN: Both connect on-premises to AWS. Direct Connect = dedicated private circuit (consistent, expensive, weeks to provision). VPN = IPsec over internet (quick setup, variable latency). For compliance requiring data NOT to traverse public internet: choose Direct Connect.
sections.contentVi: Bản đồ từ khóa cho kỳ thi: 'Dedicated private line / không qua internet' → Direct Connect; 'Encrypted tunnel qua internet' → Site-to-Site VPN; 'Hub nhiều VPC / transitive routing' → Transit Gateway (VPC Peering không hỗ trợ transitive); 'Cache nội dung tĩnh toàn cầu' → CloudFront CDN; 'IP tĩnh single entry point / tăng tốc nội dung động' → Global Accelerator; 'HTTP path-based routing / microservices' → ALB; 'Ultra-low latency TCP/UDP / hàng tỷ req/s' → NLB; 'Tường lửa bên thứ ba / IDS/IPS' → GWLB; 'DNS + đăng ký domain' → Route 53; 'Truy cập riêng tư S3/DynamoDB không qua internet' → VPC Gateway Endpoint; 'RESTful API / WebSocket managed' → API Gateway. Direct Connect vs VPN: cả hai kết nối on-premises với AWS. Direct Connect = đường riêng (ổn định, đắt, mất nhiều tuần). VPN = IPsec qua internet (cài đặt nhanh, độ trễ không ổn định). Yêu cầu compliance không cho phép dữ liệu qua internet công cộng: chọn Direct Connect.

summary: Amazon VPC is the private network foundation — by default fully isolated; add Internet Gateway for internet access, Virtual Private Gateway for on-premises
ELB types: ALB (Layer 7, HTTP/path routing), NLB (Layer 4, TCP/UDP, ultra-low latency), GWLB (Layer 3, third-party appliances), CLB (legacy)
Route 53 is the managed DNS service with routing policies: failover, latency, geolocation, geoproximity, weighted, multivalue
CloudFront caches static content at global edge locations; Global Accelerator accelerates dynamic content via the AWS backbone without caching
Direct Connect = dedicated private line (consistent, no public internet); VPN = IPsec over public internet; Transit Gateway = hub connecting hundreds of VPCs and hybrid networks
summaryVi: Amazon VPC là nền tảng mạng riêng tư — mặc định cô lập hoàn toàn; thêm Internet Gateway để có internet, Virtual Private Gateway để kết nối on-premises
Các loại ELB: ALB (Layer 7, HTTP/path routing), NLB (Layer 4, TCP/UDP, độ trễ cực thấp), GWLB (Layer 3, virtual appliances bên thứ ba), CLB (legacy)
Route 53 là dịch vụ DNS managed với các routing policy: failover, latency, geolocation, geoproximity, weighted, multivalue
CloudFront cache nội dung tĩnh tại edge locations toàn cầu; Global Accelerator tăng tốc nội dung động qua backbone AWS không cache
Direct Connect = đường riêng tư (ổn định, không qua internet); VPN = IPsec qua internet; Transit Gateway = hub kết nối hàng trăm VPC và mạng hybrid

quiz.question: A company needs to connect its on-premises data center to AWS with a dedicated, private network connection that provides consistent bandwidth and does NOT traverse the public internet. Which service meets this requirement?
options: AWS Site-to-Site VPN with IPsec
AWS Direct Connect
AWS PrivateLink Interface Endpoint
Amazon CloudFront with origin failover
quiz.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides a dedicated private circuit from on-premises to AWS that never traverses the public internet, offering consistent bandwidth. Site-to-Site VPN also connects on-premises to AWS but uses IPsec encryption over the public internet. PrivateLink is for private access to AWS services within VPCs, not on-premises connectivity. CloudFront is a CDN.

quiz.question: An e-commerce application receives HTTP and HTTPS traffic. The architecture team needs to route requests to different microservices based on the URL path (/orders goes to the orders service, /products goes to the catalog service). Which load balancer type supports this routing?
options: Classic Load Balancer
Network Load Balancer
Application Load Balancer
Gateway Load Balancer
quiz.explanation: Application Load Balancer (ALB) operates at Layer 7 and supports path-based routing, host-based routing, and header-based routing — making it the right choice for microservices. Network Load Balancer operates at Layer 4 (TCP/UDP) and does not inspect HTTP paths. Classic Load Balancer is legacy. Gateway Load Balancer is for third-party virtual appliances.

quiz.question: A global gaming company needs to serve players worldwide with minimum latency. The application uses TCP connections and the security team requires static IP addresses as a single fixed entry point. Which service BEST meets these requirements?
options: Amazon CloudFront with custom TCP origins
AWS Global Accelerator
Amazon Route 53 with latency-based routing
Network Load Balancer in each region
quiz.explanation: AWS Global Accelerator provides static Anycast IP addresses and routes traffic over the AWS global backbone network to the nearest healthy endpoint — reducing latency for TCP/UDP applications. It is NOT a caching service. CloudFront caches content at edge locations but is optimized for HTTP/HTTPS, not arbitrary TCP. Route 53 alone doesn't provide the AWS backbone acceleration. Multiple regional NLBs still require clients to resolve to the correct endpoint.

quiz.question: A media company stores large video files and images in Amazon S3. The files are accessed by users worldwide. The company wants to reduce latency and origin server load by serving content from locations close to users. Which service is the MOST cost-effective solution?
options: AWS Global Accelerator with S3 as origin
Amazon CloudFront with S3 as origin
S3 Cross-Region Replication to all regions
AWS Transit Gateway with regional S3 buckets
quiz.explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a CDN that caches static content (images, video) at edge locations globally, reducing latency and origin server load. CloudFront with S3 origin is the standard and most cost-effective pattern for this use case. Global Accelerator does not cache content, so it doesn't reduce origin load. Cross-Region Replication duplicates data but doesn't cache at edge. Transit Gateway is for network connectivity between VPCs, not content delivery.

quiz.question: A company has 60 VPCs across multiple AWS accounts and regions that all need to communicate with each other and with an on-premises network via Direct Connect. Which approach provides the LEAST operational overhead?
options: VPC Peering connections between all 60 VPCs
AWS PrivateLink endpoints for each cross-VPC service
AWS Transit Gateway connected to all VPCs and the Direct Connect Gateway
A hub VPC with peering connections to all spoke VPCs
quiz.explanation: AWS Transit Gateway acts as a regional hub that connects VPCs, VPNs, and Direct Connect Gateways through a single managed service — eliminating the need for a complex mesh of peering connections. VPC Peering is non-transitive: 60 VPCs would require up to 1,770 peering connections. PrivateLink is for service-level private endpoints, not full VPC routing. A hub-VPC with peering still requires many connections and doesn't natively support Direct Connect.

quiz.question: A financial services application must process millions of TCP/UDP packets per second with ultra-low latency. The security team requires a static IP address for whitelisting in corporate firewalls. Which Elastic Load Balancer type is MOST appropriate?
options: Application Load Balancer
Classic Load Balancer
Gateway Load Balancer
Network Load Balancer
quiz.explanation: Network Load Balancer operates at Layer 4, supports TCP/UDP/TLS, handles billions of requests per second with ultra-low latency, and supports static IP addresses — making it ideal for high-performance network workloads. ALB operates at Layer 7 (HTTP) and cannot handle raw TCP/UDP at this scale. GWLB is for deploying third-party virtual appliances. CLB is legacy and lacks modern features.

quiz.question: A company's DNS is managed in Amazon Route 53. Their primary application runs in us-east-1. In the event that us-east-1 becomes unavailable, traffic should automatically redirect to a standby environment in eu-west-1. Which Route 53 routing policy implements this?
options: Weighted routing with 50/50 distribution
Latency-based routing
Failover routing
Geolocation routing
quiz.explanation: Failover routing configures an active/passive setup: Route 53 sends traffic to the primary endpoint and automatically switches to the secondary when the primary fails its health check. Weighted routing distributes traffic by percentage — it won't automatically redirect all traffic during failure. Latency-based routing routes to the lowest-latency region, not for DR failover. Geolocation routing routes based on user geography, not resource health.

quiz.question: A solutions architect needs to deploy next-generation firewall appliances from a third-party vendor to inspect all traffic flowing between the internet and an application VPC. Which load balancer enables this architecture?
options: Application Load Balancer
Network Load Balancer
Gateway Load Balancer
Classic Load Balancer
quiz.explanation: Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB) operates at Layer 3 and is specifically designed to deploy, scale, and run third-party virtual network appliances such as firewalls, intrusion detection/prevention systems, and deep packet inspection systems.
