sections.contentVi: SSM Parameter Store so với Secrets Manager — điểm quyết định phổ biến trong đề thi: Parameter Store dùng cho dữ liệu cấu hình, biến môi trường, chuỗi không nhạy cảm; SecureString cho giá trị mã hóa; không có tính năng tự động xoay vòng sẵn có. Secrets Manager dùng cho secrets với tự động xoay vòng sẵn có; tích hợp native với RDS/Redshift/DocumentDB; chi phí cao hơn. Luồng tự động khắc phục AWS Config: Config rule phát hiện tài nguyên không tuân thủ (ví dụ S3 bucket công khai); Config kích hoạt SSM Automation Document như hành động khắc phục; Automation Document thực thi khắc phục (ví dụ tắt public access trên S3). Config đánh giá tuân thủ nhưng KHÔNG tự động sửa — cần cấu hình SSM Automation Document rõ ràng để khắc phục.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Key Governance Differentiators
sections.contentHtml:
• SSM Session Manager = secure shell access without SSH/bastion; no inbound port 22 required
  • SSM Parameter Store SecureString = encrypted config values with KMS; no auto-rotation
  • AWS Config = compliance audit and drift detection; remediation via SSM Automation
  • AWS RAM = share resources (Transit Gateway, subnets) across accounts; eliminate duplicates
  • SCPs (via Organizations/Control Tower) = preventive, deny-based; cannot grant permissions beyond identity policies
  • Control Tower guardrails = SCPs (preventive) + Config rules (detective)
  • Service Catalog = approved product catalog; users deploy without full AWS console access
Common exam pattern: "LEAST privilege / centrally managed / multiple accounts" → AWS Organizations + SCPs. "Standardize deployments across teams" → Service Catalog. "Automated multi-account setup" → Control Tower.
sections.contentVi: SSM Session Manager = shell access an toàn không cần SSH/bastion; không cần mở cổng 22. SSM Parameter Store SecureString = giá trị cấu hình mã hóa bằng KMS; không tự động xoay vòng. AWS Config = kiểm tra tuân thủ và phát hiện drift; khắc phục qua SSM Automation. AWS RAM = chia sẻ tài nguyên (Transit Gateway, subnets) giữa các tài khoản; loại bỏ tài nguyên trùng lặp. SCPs (qua Organizations/Control Tower) = phòng ngừa, từ chối; không thể cấp quyền vượt quá identity policies. Control Tower guardrails = SCPs (phòng ngừa) + Config rules (phát hiện). Service Catalog = catalog sản phẩm được phê duyệt; người dùng triển khai không cần toàn quyền AWS. Pattern phổ biến trong đề: 'quyền tối thiểu / quản lý tập trung / nhiều tài khoản' → Organizations + SCPs; 'chuẩn hóa triển khai giữa các nhóm' → Service Catalog; 'thiết lập đa tài khoản tự động' → Control Tower.

summary: SSM provides Session Manager (no-SSH shell access), Patch Manager, State Manager, and Parameter Store (SecureString=KMS-encrypted config values) for EC2 and on-premises management
AWS Config tracks configuration compliance and triggers SSM Automation Documents for auto-remediation; AWS RAM shares resources (Transit Gateway, subnets) across accounts
AWS Organizations enables consolidated billing, SCPs for access control, and central CloudTrail logging across multiple accounts
Control Tower automates multi-account landing zone setup with guardrails (SCPs=preventive + Config rules=detective); Service Catalog provides approved product catalogs for standardized deployments
summaryVi: SSM cung cấp Session Manager (shell access không cần SSH), Patch Manager, State Manager và Parameter Store (SecureString=giá trị cấu hình mã hóa KMS) cho quản lý EC2 và on-premises
AWS Config theo dõi tuân thủ cấu hình và kích hoạt SSM Automation Documents để tự động khắc phục; AWS RAM chia sẻ tài nguyên (Transit Gateway, subnets) giữa các tài khoản
AWS Organizations cho phép billing tập trung, SCPs để kiểm soát truy cập và logging CloudTrail tập trung trên nhiều tài khoản
Control Tower tự động thiết lập landing zone đa tài khoản với guardrails (SCPs=phòng ngừa + Config rules=phát hiện); Service Catalog cung cấp catalog sản phẩm được phê duyệt cho triển khai chuẩn hóa

quiz.question: A security team requires that all EC2 instances in a private subnet can be accessed for troubleshooting without opening port 22 (SSH) in security groups or deploying a bastion host. Which solution meets this requirement with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Deploy a bastion host in a public subnet and restrict SSH access via security group rules
Use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to establish shell sessions to the instances
Configure AWS VPN to allow engineers to connect directly to private subnet IPs
Enable AWS Direct Connect for secure low-latency access to the private instances
quiz.explanation: AWS Systems Manager Session Manager provides shell access to EC2 instances (and on-premises servers) without requiring SSH, open inbound ports, or a bastion host. The SSM Agent on the instance communicates outbound to the SSM service. A bastion host requires managing an additional EC2 instance, security groups, and SSH keys — significant operational overhead. VPN and Direct Connect provide network connectivity but still require SSH/port 22 once connected.

quiz.question: A company stores AMI IDs, database connection strings, and environment-specific configuration in SSM Parameter Store. Some values contain sensitive information. Which Parameter Store type ensures these sensitive values are encrypted at rest?
options: Standard String — default type; values are stored in plaintext
SecureString — values are encrypted using an AWS KMS CMK
EncryptedString — uses AES-256 encryption managed by SSM
ConfidentialString — requires MFA to retrieve the value
quiz.explanation: SSM Parameter Store's SecureString type encrypts the stored value using an AWS KMS CMK. Applications retrieve the decrypted value at runtime via the SSM API (with appropriate IAM permissions). Standard String stores values in plaintext and is suitable for non-sensitive configuration. 'EncryptedString' and 'ConfidentialString' are not valid Parameter Store types.

quiz.question: An operations team needs to automate OS patching for 200 EC2 instances across multiple regions on a defined maintenance schedule. Which AWS service provides this capability with LEAST operational overhead?
options: AWS Config with a custom rule that checks for missing patches and triggers Lambda
AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager with scheduled maintenance windows
AWS OpsWorks with Chef recipes for automated patching
Deploy a cron job on each EC2 instance that runs package manager updates
quiz.explanation: AWS Systems Manager Patch Manager automates the patching of managed instances across accounts and regions. It supports maintenance windows for scheduling patches during defined periods with no manual intervention per instance. Config with Lambda could detect unpatched instances but is not designed for executing the patching process. OpsWorks can manage configurations but requires more setup and maintenance than Patch Manager. Cron jobs per instance require managing scripts on each instance individually — significant operational overhead at scale.

quiz.question: An organization with 30 AWS accounts needs to ensure no account can ever disable AWS CloudTrail logging. The security team wants a preventive control enforced across all accounts from a central location. Which solution BEST meets this requirement?
options: Deploy an AWS Config rule in each account that detects when CloudTrail is disabled
Use AWS Organizations Service Control Policies (SCPs) to deny the cloudtrail:StopLogging action across all accounts
Enable AWS Control Tower guardrails to detect CloudTrail violations using Config rules
Configure IAM permission boundaries in each account to prevent CloudTrail modification
quiz.explanation: AWS Organizations SCPs are preventive controls applied at the organization or OU level. An SCP denying cloudtrail:StopLogging will prevent any principal in any member account from disabling CloudTrail, regardless of their IAM permissions. Config rules (option A and C's detective guardrails) are detective controls — they detect violations after the fact but do not prevent the action. IAM permission boundaries only constrain what IAM entities can do within a single account and require management in each account individually.

quiz.question: A company has S3 buckets that should never be publicly accessible. They want to automatically detect and remediate any bucket that is accidentally made public. Which combination of AWS services achieves this with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Enable Amazon Macie to detect public buckets and trigger a Lambda function to block public access
Use AWS Config with a managed rule for public S3 buckets and configure an SSM Automation Document for auto-remediation
Schedule a Lambda function to run every hour and check all S3 bucket ACLs
Deploy Amazon GuardDuty S3 protection and configure findings to trigger remediation
quiz.explanation: AWS Config has managed rules (like s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited) that evaluate S3 bucket compliance. When a bucket becomes public, Config detects the violation and can trigger an SSM Automation Document (e.g., AWS-DisableS3BucketPublicReadWrite) to automatically remediate it. This is a purpose-built, fully managed approach. Macie focuses on sensitive data discovery, not bucket ACL compliance enforcement. A scheduled Lambda function introduces unnecessary delay and requires custom code to maintain. GuardDuty S3 protection detects data access threats, not configuration compliance issues like public ACLs.

quiz.question: A company has multiple AWS accounts and wants to share a centrally managed Transit Gateway across all accounts without creating separate Transit Gateways in each account. Which AWS service enables this?
options: AWS Organizations with consolidated billing to share network resources
AWS Resource Access Manager (RAM) to share the Transit Gateway across accounts
VPC Peering between each account's VPCs
AWS PrivateLink endpoints in each account connecting to the central Transit Gateway
quiz.explanation: AWS Resource Access Manager (RAM) enables sharing of AWS resources like Transit Gateways, subnets, and Route 53 Resolver rules across accounts or within an AWS Organization. This eliminates the need to create duplicate Transit Gateways in each account, reducing cost and operational overhead. AWS Organizations with consolidated billing does not share network resources. VPC Peering requires a mesh of peering connections and does not scale well for many accounts. PrivateLink is for sharing services (not Transit Gateways) via endpoint services.

quiz.question: A platform engineering team wants developers to provision pre-approved infrastructure stacks (e.g., a 3-tier web app template, a data lake template) without giving them broad AWS console access. Which AWS service BEST meets this requirement?
options: AWS CloudFormation with IAM roles — developers run stacks using a shared role
AWS Service Catalog — publish approved CloudFormation templates as products in portfolios
AWS Control Tower with a custom blueprint for each approved stack type
AWS Systems Manager with Automation Documents for each infrastructure pattern
quiz.explanation: AWS Service Catalog allows the platform team to publish approved CloudFormation templates as products organized in portfolios, and grant developers access to launch only those products. Developers can deploy compliant infrastructure without broad IAM permissions. CloudFormation with a shared role allows deployment but provides no catalog structure, access control by product, or self-service browsing of approved options. Control Tower manages account provisioning and governance, not application-level infrastructure catalogs. SSM Automation Documents are for operational tasks, not infrastructure provisioning as a service.

quiz.question: A company is setting up a new multi-account AWS environment following AWS best practices. They want automated account vending, pre-configured security guardrails, and a landing zone with minimal manual setup. Which service is MOST appropriate?
options: AWS Organizations with manually configured SCPs and CloudTrail
AWS Control Tower for automated landing zone setup with built-in guardrails
AWS Config with organization-wide rules deployed via CloudFormation StackSets
AWS Service Catalog with an account provisioning product
quiz.explanation: AWS Control Tower is purpose-built for automated multi-account setup. It uses blueprints (AWS best practices) to create a landing zone, automatically sets up AWS Organizations, enables CloudTrail, deploys Config rules, and implements guardrails (SCPs for preventive, Config rules for detective) across all accounts. AWS Organizations requires manual configuration of all policies and guardrails. Config with StackSets adds compliance rules but does not provision accounts or set up a landing zone. Service Catalog can automate account provisioning but requires custom products and does not provide the full governance framework that Control Tower delivers.

quiz.question: A company uses AWS Organizations and wants to implement governance across all member accounts. They need: (1) a preventive control that blocks member accounts from creating resources in regions outside us-east-1 and eu-west-1, and (2) a detective control that identifies EC2 instances running unapproved AMIs. (Choose TWO)
options: SCP attached to the root OU denying actions where the requested region is not us-east-1 or eu-west-1
AWS Config managed rule checking EC2 instances against an approved AMI list
AWS Config managed rule denying resource creation in non-approved regions
SCP that triggers a Lambda function to validate AMI IDs before EC2 launch
AWS CloudTrail with an EventBridge rule to detect non-approved AMI usage
quiz.explanation: An SCP (option A) denying API calls where the condition aws:RequestedRegion is not in [us-east-1, eu-west-1] provides a preventive control that blocks resource creation in all other regions across the organization. An AWS Config rule (option B) like approved-amis-by-id evaluates running EC2 instances against an approved list — this is a detective control. Config rules cannot deny/prevent actions (option C is incorrect — Config is detective, not preventive). SCPs cannot trigger Lambda for AMI validation (option D is not how SCPs work). CloudTrail + EventBridge (option E) can detect and potentially respond to violations but requires custom Lambda code and is more complex than a managed Config rule.

quiz.question: A company needs to ensure that EC2 instance configurations remain consistent with a defined baseline over time. When an instance drifts from the baseline (e.g., a required agent is uninstalled), it should be automatically brought back into compliance. Which SSM feature addresses this?
options: SSM Session Manager — connect to drifted instances and manually re-apply configurations
SSM State Manager — define desired state associations that continuously enforce configuration
SSM Patch Manager — apply patches to bring instances back to the defined baseline
SSM Parameter Store — store the baseline configuration and trigger Lambda on drift detection
quiz.explanation: SSM State Manager allows you to define desired configuration states (e.g., agents installed, security settings applied) and associate them with instances. State Manager periodically checks compliance and automatically re-applies the configuration if drift is detected, supporting Ansible, Chef, and PowerShell runbooks. Session Manager is for interactive shell access and cannot enforce configuration. Patch Manager handles OS patches, not configuration state management. Parameter Store is a configuration data store and does not actively enforce instance configuration.

quiz.question: A company uses AWS Organizations and wants volume pricing discounts on AWS services to be shared across all member accounts. Which AWS Organizations feature enables this?
options: Service Control Policies (SCPs) with a discount-sharing permission
Consolidated billing — a single bill across all accounts with volume discount aggregation
AWS Cost Explorer with cross-account saving plan sharing
AWS Budgets with an aggregated billing alert across all accounts
quiz.explanation: AWS Organizations consolidated billing aggregates usage across all member accounts for billing purposes. This means volume discounts and tiered pricing thresholds are calculated against the total combined usage, benefiting all accounts. SCPs are access control policies, not billing features. Cost Explorer provides visibility but does not enable discount sharing. AWS Budgets sets spend alerts but does not change how discounts are applied.

quiz.question: An AWS Config rule detects that an S3 bucket has been made publicly readable. The security team wants Config to automatically fix the violation without any manual intervention. How is this automated remediation configured?
options: Enable AWS Config 'auto-fix mode' in the console to apply corrections automatically
Configure an SSM Automation Document as the remediation action for the Config rule
Create an EventBridge rule that triggers a Lambda function when Config detects violations
Set the Config rule to DENY mode so it blocks the non-compliant change in real time
quiz.explanation: AWS Config supports auto-remediation by associating an SSM Automation Document with a Config rule as the remediation action. When the rule evaluates a resource as non-compliant, Config can automatically trigger the Automation Document (e.g., AWS-DisableS3BucketPublicReadWrite). There is no 'auto-fix mode' in Config. EventBridge + Lambda (option C) also works and is valid, but Config's built-in remediation via SSM Automation is the purpose-built, lower-overhead approach. Config rules are detective — they cannot block changes in real time (option D is wrong).

quiz.question: A DevOps team wants to enforce configuration standards on newly launched EC2 instances — specifically, ensuring that a custom monitoring agent is always installed within minutes of instance launch. Which AWS Systems Manager capability is MOST appropriate?
options: SSM Patch Manager with a baseline that includes the monitoring agent
SSM State Manager with an association that runs the agent installation document on all new instances
SSM Session Manager to manually install the agent on each new instance
SSM Run Command to execute a one-time install script on all instances at launch
quiz.explanation: SSM State Manager creates persistent associations between instances and configuration documents. When new instances register with SSM, State Manager automatically applies the association (e.g., installs the monitoring agent). It continuously enforces the desired state. Patch Manager handles OS patches, not custom agent installation. Session Manager is for interactive access, not automated enforcement. Run Command executes one-time commands and does not continuously enforce state on new instances.

quiz.question: A company wants to track all configuration changes to their AWS resources over the past 90 days and determine when a specific security group rule was removed. Which AWS service provides this historical configuration change tracking?
options: AWS CloudTrail — query API call history for security group modifications
AWS Config — review the configuration history timeline for the security group resource
Amazon CloudWatch Logs — search log streams for security group change events
AWS Systems Manager — review the operational history for the security group
quiz.explanation: AWS Config maintains a configuration history and timeline for each tracked resource. You can view the exact state of a security group at any point in time and see when specific rules were added or removed. CloudTrail records the API calls (who made the change and when) but shows API-level events rather than a resource-centric configuration timeline — Config is the better tool for 'what did this resource look like at time T.' CloudWatch Logs captures log data but not structured resource configuration history. Systems Manager does not track resource configuration changes.

quiz.question: A large enterprise is setting up AWS Control Tower for their multi-account environment. The governance team asks about guardrails. Which TWO statements about Control Tower guardrails are correct? (Choose TWO)
options: Preventive guardrails are implemented using Service Control Policies (SCPs) and block non-compliant actions
Detective guardrails are implemented using AWS Config rules and identify policy violations
Guardrails are optional and can be disabled per account without affecting the management account
Detective guardrails use SCPs to report on non-compliant resources without blocking them
Preventive guardrails use AWS Config rules to evaluate resources and generate findings
quiz.explanation: Control Tower guardrails come in two types: preventive guardrails (option A) use SCPs to deny non-compliant API actions before they occur; detective guardrails (option B) use AWS Config rules to identify resources that violate policies after the fact. Option C is partially true for optional guardrails but misleading — mandatory guardrails cannot be disabled. Option D is incorrect — detective guardrails use Config rules, not SCPs. Option E is incorrect — preventive guardrails use SCPs, not Config rules.

quiz.question: A company wants developers in multiple teams to be able to deploy standardized data lake infrastructure without requiring deep AWS expertise. The platform team will define and approve the templates. Access must be limited to only the approved templates. Which service BEST meets this requirement?
options: AWS CloudFormation StackSets — deploy templates across accounts automatically
AWS Service Catalog — publish approved templates as products in team-specific portfolios
AWS Control Tower — use blueprints to define standard data lake landing zones
AWS Systems Manager Automation Documents — create runbooks for data lake provisioning
quiz.explanation: AWS Service Catalog is purpose-built for this use case. The platform team publishes CloudFormation-based products into portfolios, grants specific teams access to their portfolio, and developers can only launch the approved products without needing access to the underlying CloudFormation service or broad IAM permissions. StackSets are for deploying the same stack to multiple accounts/regions — they do not provide a self-service catalog interface. Control Tower manages account-level governance and landing zones, not application-level infrastructure products. SSM Automation Documents are for operational runbooks, not infrastructure provisioning self-service.

lessons.title: AWS Identity Services
lessons.overview: Covers the four core AWS identity services — IAM, Amazon Cognito, AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO), and AWS Directory Service — focusing on authentication vs. authorization patterns, federation, and when to use each service.
lessons.overviewVi: Tổng quan bốn dịch vụ identity AWS cốt lõi — IAM, Amazon Cognito, AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO) và AWS Directory Service — tập trung vào các pattern xác thực vs. ủy quyền, liên kết và khi nào dùng từng dịch vụ.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Identity in AWS: Authentication vs. Authorization
sections.contentHtml:
AWS identity services handle two distinct concerns:

AuthenticationWho are you? — Cognito User Pools, IAM, Directory Service, IAM Identity Center
AuthorizationWhat can you do? — IAM policies, SCPs, Cognito Identity Pools (temporary AWS credentials)

Understanding which service handles which concern — and how they chain together — is critical for SAA-C03 architecture questions.

Key rule: Cognito User Pools = authentication (sign-in); Cognito Identity Pools = authorization (AWS credentials). IAM = internal AWS access control. IAM Identity Center = SSO across accounts and apps.
sections.contentVi: Các dịch vụ identity AWS xử lý hai mối quan tâm riêng biệt: Authentication (Xác thực) — bạn là ai? — Cognito User Pools, IAM, Directory Service, IAM Identity Center. Authorization (Ủy quyền) — bạn có thể làm gì? — IAM policies, SCPs, Cognito Identity Pools (temporary AWS credentials). Hiểu dịch vụ nào xử lý mối quan tâm nào và cách chúng kết hợp với nhau là rất quan trọng cho các câu hỏi kiến trúc SAA-C03. Quy tắc chính: Cognito User Pools = xác thực (đăng nhập); Cognito Identity Pools = ủy quyền (AWS credentials tạm thời); IAM = kiểm soát truy cập AWS nội bộ; IAM Identity Center = SSO trên nhiều tài khoản và ứng dụng.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: AWS IAM
sections.contentHtml:
AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) is the primary AWS identity service for controlling access to AWS resources.

IAM UsersLong-term credentials: username/password for console + access key ID/secret access key for CLI/API
IAM GroupsCollection of users; assign policies to a group to apply to all members
IAM PoliciesJSON documents defining Allow/Deny for specific actions on specific resources
IAM RolesRole-based identity for users, accounts, or AWS services; provides temporary credentials via STS
Note: IAM Roles are preferred over IAM Users for AWS service-to-service access (e.g., EC2 accessing S3). Roles provide temporary credentials, eliminating the need for long-term access keys.
sections.contentVi: AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) là dịch vụ identity AWS chính để kiểm soát truy cập vào tài nguyên AWS. IAM Users: credentials dài hạn — username/password cho console + access key ID/secret access key cho CLI/API. IAM Groups: tập hợp users; gán policies cho group để áp dụng cho tất cả thành viên. IAM Policies: tài liệu JSON định nghĩa Allow/Deny cho hành động cụ thể trên tài nguyên cụ thể. IAM Roles: identity dựa trên role cho users, accounts hoặc dịch vụ AWS; cung cấp temporary credentials qua STS. IAM Roles được ưu tiên hơn IAM Users cho truy cập giữa các dịch vụ AWS (ví dụ EC2 truy cập S3) — Roles cung cấp temporary credentials, loại bỏ nhu cầu long-term access keys.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon Cognito: User Pools and Identity Pools
sections.contentHtml:
Amazon Cognito provides authentication and authorization for web and mobile applications.

User Pools — Authentication:

  • Managed user directory for sign-up/sign-in
  • Supports social identity providers (Facebook, Google, Amazon) and SAML-based IdPs
  • Returns JWT tokens (ID token, access token, refresh token) upon successful authentication
  • Does NOT provide AWS service credentials

Identity Pools — Authorization:

  • Exchanges a valid identity token (from User Pool, Google, Facebook, SAML, etc.) for temporary AWS credentials via STS
  • Grants limited-privilege access to AWS services (S3, DynamoDB, API Gateway, etc.)
User PoolsAuthentication → JWT tokens; who you are
Identity PoolsAuthorization → temporary AWS credentials via STS; what you can access
Common architecture: User signs in via User Pool → gets JWT → exchanges JWT at Identity Pool → gets temporary AWS credentials → accesses S3/DynamoDB directly.
sections.contentVi: Amazon Cognito cung cấp xác thực và ủy quyền cho ứng dụng web và di động. User Pools — Xác thực: thư mục người dùng được quản lý để đăng ký/đăng nhập; hỗ trợ social IdP (Facebook, Google, Amazon) và SAML-based IdPs; trả về JWT tokens (ID token, access token, refresh token) khi xác thực thành công; KHÔNG cung cấp AWS service credentials. Identity Pools — Ủy quyền: đổi identity token hợp lệ (từ User Pool, Google, Facebook, SAML) lấy temporary AWS credentials qua STS; cấp quyền truy cập hạn chế vào dịch vụ AWS (S3, DynamoDB, API Gateway). User Pools: xác thực → JWT tokens — bạn là ai. Identity Pools: ủy quyền → temporary AWS credentials qua STS — bạn có thể truy cập gì. Kiến trúc phổ biến: người dùng đăng nhập qua User Pool → nhận JWT → đổi JWT tại Identity Pool → nhận temporary AWS credentials → truy cập trực tiếp S3/DynamoDB.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO) and Directory Service
sections.contentHtml:
AWS IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO) provides single sign-on access across multiple AWS accounts and business applications:

  • One set of credentials for access to all AWS accounts in the organization
  • AWS Access Portal (user portal) to select accounts and apps
  • Pre-configured SAML integrations for business apps (Salesforce, Office 365, etc.)
  • Integrates with Active Directory (via AWS Directory Service)

AWS Directory Service provides managed Microsoft Active Directory:

  • No need to manage AD domain controllers on EC2
  • Supports AD Connector for on-premises Active Directory integration
  • Assign IAM roles to AD users and groups
IAM Identity CenterSSO across multiple AWS accounts and SAML apps; one set of credentials
Directory ServiceManaged Microsoft AD; for EC2 domain join, AD-integrated apps, on-prem AD federation
sections.contentVi: AWS IAM Identity Center (trước đây là AWS SSO) cung cấp single sign-on trên nhiều tài khoản AWS và ứng dụng kinh doanh: một bộ credentials để truy cập tất cả tài khoản AWS trong tổ chức; AWS Access Portal để chọn tài khoản và ứng dụng; tích hợp SAML sẵn có cho ứng dụng kinh doanh (Salesforce, Office 365); tích hợp với Active Directory (qua AWS Directory Service). AWS Directory Service cung cấp Microsoft Active Directory được quản lý: không cần quản lý AD domain controllers trên EC2; hỗ trợ AD Connector để tích hợp với on-premises Active Directory; gán IAM roles cho AD users và groups. IAM Identity Center: SSO trên nhiều tài khoản AWS và SAML apps; một bộ credentials. Directory Service: managed Microsoft AD; dùng cho EC2 domain join, ứng dụng tích hợp AD, liên kết với on-prem AD.

sections.label: Deep Dive
sections.heading: Federation Patterns and When to Use Each Service
sections.contentHtml:
Different scenarios call for different identity federation patterns:

Mobile/web app users need AWS accessCognito User Pools (auth) → Identity Pools (temporary AWS credentials via STS)
Enterprise employees: SSO to multiple AWS accountsIAM Identity Center with AD integration (Directory Service/AD Connector)
EC2 instances need AWS service accessIAM Roles attached to EC2 — no long-term credentials needed
On-premises AD users need AWS console accessAD Connector + IAM Identity Center or SAML federation with IAM
Social login (Google/Facebook) for consumer appCognito User Pools as the social IdP aggregator
Key distinction: Cognito is for external/app users; IAM Identity Center is for internal/enterprise employees; IAM is for AWS-internal service access and developers.
sections.contentVi: Các tình huống khác nhau cần các pattern liên kết identity khác nhau: người dùng mobile/web cần truy cập AWS → Cognito User Pools (xác thực) → Identity Pools (temporary AWS credentials qua STS). Nhân viên doanh nghiệp: SSO đến nhiều tài khoản AWS → IAM Identity Center tích hợp AD (Directory Service/AD Connector). EC2 instances cần truy cập dịch vụ AWS → IAM Roles gắn vào EC2 — không cần long-term credentials. Người dùng on-premises AD cần truy cập AWS console → AD Connector + IAM Identity Center hoặc SAML federation với IAM. Đăng nhập mạng xã hội (Google/Facebook) cho ứng dụng người tiêu dùng → Cognito User Pools làm social IdP aggregator. Phân biệt chính: Cognito dành cho người dùng bên ngoài/ứng dụng; IAM Identity Center dành cho nhân viên nội bộ/doanh nghiệp; IAM dành cho truy cập dịch vụ AWS nội bộ và lập trình viên.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Identity Service Quick Reference
sections.contentHtml:
• IAM Users = long-term credentials; avoid for apps — use roles instead
  • IAM Roles = temporary credentials via STS; for services, cross-account, federation
  • Cognito User Pools = app user authentication → JWT tokens; NOT AWS credentials
  • Cognito Identity Pools = exchange tokens for temporary AWS credentials; federation to AWS services
  • IAM Identity Center = SSO for employees across AWS accounts + SAML business apps
  • AWS Directory Service = managed AD for EC2 domain join, AD-integrated workloads, on-prem AD extension
Common exam traps:
— Question about app users accessing S3 → Cognito (User Pools + Identity Pools), NOT IAM users
— Question about employees signing in once to access multiple AWS accounts → IAM Identity Center (SSO), NOT Cognito
— Question about EC2 needing to call S3/DynamoDB → IAM Role on EC2, NOT access keys in code
sections.contentVi: IAM Users = long-term credentials; tránh dùng cho apps — dùng roles thay thế. IAM Roles = temporary credentials qua STS; cho dịch vụ, cross-account, federation. Cognito User Pools = xác thực người dùng ứng dụng → JWT tokens; KHÔNG phải AWS credentials. Cognito Identity Pools = đổi tokens lấy temporary AWS credentials; federation đến dịch vụ AWS. IAM Identity Center = SSO cho nhân viên trên các tài khoản AWS + SAML business apps. AWS Directory Service = managed AD cho EC2 domain join, workloads tích hợp AD, mở rộng on-prem AD. Bẫy phổ biến trong đề: câu hỏi về app users truy cập S3 → Cognito (User Pools + Identity Pools), KHÔNG phải IAM users; câu hỏi về nhân viên đăng nhập một lần để truy cập nhiều tài khoản AWS → IAM Identity Center (SSO), KHÔNG phải Cognito; câu hỏi về EC2 cần gọi S3/DynamoDB → IAM Role trên EC2, KHÔNG phải access keys trong code.

summary: IAM provides users (long-term credentials), groups, policies, and roles (temporary credentials via STS) — roles are preferred over users for service-to-service access
Cognito User Pools handle authentication (JWT tokens) while Identity Pools handle authorization (temporary AWS credentials via STS) for web and mobile application users
IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO) provides single sign-on across multiple AWS accounts and SAML-integrated business applications with one set of credentials
AWS Directory Service provides managed Microsoft Active Directory; AD Connector extends on-premises AD to AWS without deploying domain controllers on EC2
summaryVi: IAM cung cấp users (long-term credentials), groups, policies và roles (temporary credentials qua STS) — roles được ưu tiên hơn users cho truy cập dịch vụ-đến-dịch vụ
Cognito User Pools xử lý xác thực (JWT tokens) trong khi Identity Pools xử lý ủy quyền (temporary AWS credentials qua STS) cho người dùng ứng dụng web và di động
IAM Identity Center (trước đây là AWS SSO) cung cấp single sign-on trên nhiều tài khoản AWS và ứng dụng kinh doanh tích hợp SAML với một bộ credentials
AWS Directory Service cung cấp Microsoft Active Directory được quản lý; AD Connector mở rộng on-premises AD đến AWS mà không cần triển khai domain controllers trên EC2

quiz.question: A mobile application requires users to sign in using their Google or Facebook accounts, and after authentication, the app needs to store user-specific files in Amazon S3 with each user only able to access their own files. Which combination of AWS services achieves this with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Create IAM users for each app user with S3 access policies scoped to their prefix
Use Amazon Cognito User Pools for authentication and Cognito Identity Pools to provide temporary AWS credentials for S3 access
Use AWS IAM Identity Center to federate Google/Facebook logins and grant S3 access
Deploy a backend API on EC2 that authenticates with Google/Facebook and uses a shared IAM role to access S3
quiz.explanation: Cognito User Pools supports social identity providers (Google, Facebook) for sign-in, issuing JWT tokens. Cognito Identity Pools exchanges those JWT tokens for temporary, scoped AWS credentials via STS, allowing the app to directly access S3 with per-user prefix-based bucket policies. Creating IAM users for each app user is not scalable and is an anti-pattern. IAM Identity Center is for enterprise employee SSO, not consumer app social login. A shared IAM role on EC2 does not provide per-user credential isolation for S3 access.

quiz.question: A company has 1,000 employees who need to access multiple AWS accounts (dev, staging, production) and business SaaS applications (Salesforce, Office 365). Employees should use their existing corporate Active Directory credentials. Which solution provides this with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Create IAM users in each AWS account and configure cross-account roles
Use AWS IAM Identity Center with AWS Directory Service to federate corporate AD credentials for SSO across accounts and apps
Configure Amazon Cognito User Pools with SAML integration to each AWS account
Deploy ADFS on EC2 and configure SAML federation with each individual AWS account
quiz.explanation: IAM Identity Center provides SSO for employees across multiple AWS accounts and pre-configured SAML integrations for business apps (Salesforce, Office 365). Integrating with AWS Directory Service or AD Connector allows employees to use their existing AD credentials. This is a single centrally managed solution. Creating IAM users in each account requires managing thousands of users across accounts — very high overhead. Cognito is for application end-user authentication, not enterprise employee SSO. ADFS on EC2 requires managing the ADFS infrastructure and configuring federation per account — high overhead.

quiz.question: A web application uses Amazon Cognito for user authentication. After a user signs in, the application needs to provide that user with temporary credentials to read objects from a specific S3 bucket. Which Cognito component provides the temporary AWS credentials?
options: Cognito User Pools — after authentication, User Pools issue AWS credentials directly
Cognito Identity Pools — exchanges the User Pool JWT token for temporary AWS credentials via STS
Cognito Sync — synchronizes user credentials across devices
Cognito Access Control — grants S3 access based on the JWT token claims
quiz.explanation: Cognito Identity Pools (Federated Identities) accept identity tokens (such as JWTs from a User Pool) and exchange them for temporary AWS credentials via AWS STS. These credentials are scoped to the IAM role associated with the authenticated identity. User Pools handle authentication and issue JWTs, but they do not issue AWS service credentials. Cognito Sync is a deprecated feature for cross-device data synchronization. 'Cognito Access Control' is not a separate Cognito component.

quiz.question: An EC2 instance needs to read objects from an S3 bucket and write results to a DynamoDB table. The application running on EC2 should access these services securely. What is the MOST secure and LEAST overhead approach?
options: Create an IAM user, generate access keys, and store them in the application's config file on EC2
Attach an IAM role to the EC2 instance with the required S3 and DynamoDB permissions
Store the AWS access keys in AWS Secrets Manager and retrieve them at application startup
Configure the application to assume an IAM role by calling STS AssumeRole at startup and caching the credentials
quiz.explanation: Attaching an IAM role to the EC2 instance is the correct approach. The EC2 instance metadata service (IMDS) automatically provides temporary credentials that the AWS SDK uses — no key management required. This is the most secure pattern because credentials are rotated automatically and never stored in code or config files. Storing access keys in a config file is a security anti-pattern (long-term keys, risk of exposure). Secrets Manager + startup retrieval adds complexity and still requires bootstrapping credentials to call Secrets Manager. Manual STS AssumeRole at startup adds unnecessary complexity when instance profiles handle this transparently.

quiz.question: A company wants to allow its on-premises Active Directory users to sign in to the AWS Management Console without creating separate IAM users in each AWS account. The AD infrastructure is on-premises. Which solution requires LEAST operational overhead?
options: Deploy AD on EC2 in AWS and set up a trust relationship with on-premises AD
Configure AWS Directory Service AD Connector with IAM Identity Center to federate on-premises AD credentials
Create IAM users matching each AD account and configure IAM roles with SAML trust policies
Use Amazon Cognito User Pools with on-premises LDAP as a custom identity provider
quiz.explanation: AD Connector is a gateway that connects AWS Directory Service to the existing on-premises AD without caching or syncing data to AWS. Combined with IAM Identity Center, on-premises AD users can SSO into the AWS console using their existing credentials. No new AD infrastructure in AWS is needed. Deploying AD on EC2 requires provisioning and managing EC2 instances for domain controllers. Creating IAM users defeats the purpose of federating existing AD users. Cognito is for application user authentication, not employee console access.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is designing an identity strategy for a new SaaS application with the following requirements: (1) end users should sign in using Google or Amazon accounts, (2) after sign-in, users should have temporary AWS credentials to upload files to a personal S3 prefix. Which TWO services are required? (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon Cognito User Pools to handle social sign-in (Google, Amazon) and issue JWT tokens
AWS IAM Identity Center to federate Google and Amazon social identities
Amazon Cognito Identity Pools to exchange JWT tokens for temporary AWS credentials via STS
AWS Directory Service to manage the social identity provider connections
IAM Users created per application user to provide S3 access
quiz.explanation: Cognito User Pools (option A) supports Google and Amazon as social identity providers, handling the sign-in flow and issuing JWT tokens. Cognito Identity Pools (option C) accepts those JWT tokens and exchanges them for temporary AWS credentials via STS, with the credentials scoped to an IAM role that allows access to the user's S3 prefix. IAM Identity Center is for enterprise employee SSO across AWS accounts, not consumer social login. Directory Service manages Microsoft AD, not social identity providers. Creating IAM users per application user is not scalable and is an anti-pattern.

quiz.question: A developer is asked what the difference is between an IAM User and an IAM Role. Which statement BEST distinguishes them for a solutions architect exam context?
options: IAM Users are for humans and IAM Roles are only for AWS services like Lambda and EC2
IAM Users have permanent long-term credentials; IAM Roles provide temporary credentials via STS and can be assumed by users, services, or external accounts
IAM Roles provide permanent credentials that are rotated every 90 days by default
IAM Users and Roles have identical functionality; the difference is only organizational
quiz.explanation: IAM Users have permanent long-term credentials (password + access keys) that persist until explicitly deleted or rotated. IAM Roles provide temporary credentials issued by STS, and they can be assumed by IAM users, AWS services (EC2, Lambda), or external identities via federation. Roles are the preferred mechanism for granting access because temporary credentials expire automatically. Option A is incorrect — roles can also be assumed by human users. Option C is incorrect — role credentials are temporary (hours) and not 'permanent credentials rotated every 90 days'. Option D is incorrect — they have fundamentally different credential models.

quiz.question: A company uses Windows-based workloads that require Active Directory for authentication, group policy, and domain join. They want to migrate these workloads to AWS EC2 without managing AD domain controllers on EC2. Which AWS service provides managed Active Directory?
options: AWS IAM Identity Center — provides managed AD for EC2 domain join
AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD)
AWS Secrets Manager — stores AD service account credentials for domain join
Amazon Cognito with SAML federation to connect to AD
quiz.explanation: AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD) provides a fully managed AD in AWS without the need to deploy or manage domain controller EC2 instances. EC2 instances can be domain-joined to the managed AD, enabling group policy, Kerberos authentication, and AD-integrated application support. IAM Identity Center uses Directory Service under the hood for AD integration but is not itself a directory service for EC2 domain join. Secrets Manager stores credentials but does not provide an AD directory. Cognito with SAML is for application-level federated authentication, not AD domain join.

quiz.question: A company needs to grant a third-party auditing firm temporary read-only access to specific AWS resources in their account. The firm has their own AWS account. Which is the MOST secure approach that requires LEAST operational overhead?
options: Create an IAM user in the company's account, generate access keys, and share them with the auditing firm
Create an IAM role in the company's account with a cross-account trust policy allowing the auditing firm's AWS account to assume it
Add the auditing firm's IAM users to a group in the company's account with read-only permissions
Create a temporary IAM user, set a password expiration for 30 days, and delete the user after the audit
quiz.explanation: Creating an IAM role with a cross-account trust policy that allows the auditing firm's AWS account (principal) to assume the role via STS is the most secure approach. The role provides temporary credentials that expire automatically, and no long-term access keys need to be shared. The company can revoke access at any time by modifying the trust policy. Sharing IAM user access keys (option A) is a security risk — long-term credentials can be leaked. You cannot add external account users to a group in your account (option C — cross-account access requires roles, not group membership). Temporary IAM users (option D) still use long-term credentials and require manual cleanup.

quiz.question: A startup is building a consumer-facing fitness application. Users should be able to register with their email, and the app will store fitness data accessible only to each individual user in DynamoDB. Which AWS services should be used for user authentication and AWS resource authorization?
options: IAM users for each app user with DynamoDB fine-grained access control
Amazon Cognito User Pools for registration/authentication and Cognito Identity Pools for temporary DynamoDB access credentials
AWS IAM Identity Center with SAML integration to the app's user database
Amazon Cognito User Pools only — User Pools provide both JWT tokens and AWS credentials
quiz.explanation: Cognito User Pools manage user registration, login, and MFA, issuing JWT tokens upon successful authentication. Cognito Identity Pools exchange those JWT tokens for temporary AWS credentials scoped to an IAM role. The IAM role can use DynamoDB condition keys (like cognito-identity.amazonaws.com:sub) to restrict each user to their own data — fine-grained authorization. IAM users per app user is not scalable for a consumer application. IAM Identity Center is for enterprise employee SSO. User Pools alone do not provide AWS service credentials — that is the role of Identity Pools.

quiz.question: An organization uses AWS IAM Identity Center. Employees use the Access Portal to select the AWS account they want to work in. What best describes the Access Portal in this context?
options: A custom-built portal the company must develop to integrate with IAM Identity Center
The AWS-provided user portal (web UI) where employees see all accounts and apps they have access to and can sign in with one set of credentials
The IAM console in the management account that shows cross-account role assumptions
A CloudFront-hosted portal that requires separate authentication for each AWS account
quiz.explanation: AWS IAM Identity Center includes a built-in AWS Access Portal — a web interface automatically provided by AWS. After a single sign-on with their credentials, employees see all the AWS accounts and applications (Salesforce, Office 365, etc.) they have been granted access to and can launch them with one click. No custom development is required. The IAM console shows IAM-level policies, not a user-friendly SSO portal. It does not require separate authentication per account — that is the whole point of SSO.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is reviewing an identity architecture for a company with the following requirements: all EC2 instances in production must NOT use IAM user credentials, and external partners must have time-limited access to read from an S3 bucket. Which TWO solutions satisfy these requirements? (Choose TWO)
options: Attach IAM roles to EC2 instances to provide temporary credentials automatically via instance metadata
Create IAM users for EC2 applications and rotate their access keys every 30 days
Create a cross-account IAM role in the company's account with an S3 read policy and a trust policy for the partner's AWS account
Create IAM users for external partners and set an account password expiration policy
Store EC2 application credentials in AWS Secrets Manager and retrieve at instance launch
quiz.explanation: Attaching IAM roles to EC2 instances (option A) provides automatic, rotating temporary credentials via the instance metadata service — no IAM user credentials on EC2. Creating a cross-account IAM role (option C) allows external partners to assume the role via STS using their own AWS account identity, receiving time-limited temporary credentials for S3 read access. IAM users on EC2 (option B) uses long-term credentials — violates the first requirement. IAM users for partners (option D) provides long-term credentials, not time-limited access. Secrets Manager (option E) still stores credentials and does not address the 'no IAM user credentials' requirement for EC2.

quiz.question: A company's application developers need to assume different IAM roles depending on the environment they are working in (dev, staging, production) from a single IAM user identity. Which IAM capability enables this?
options: IAM Groups — add the developer to dev, staging, and production groups with different policies
IAM Role assumption — developers call STS AssumeRole to switch to the appropriate environment role using their IAM user credentials
IAM Permission Boundaries — set boundaries that allow access to all environments simultaneously
IAM Policy Conditions — add condition keys that grant access to all environments from one policy
quiz.explanation: IAM role assumption (STS AssumeRole) is the correct pattern. Each developer has a single IAM user identity. When working on a specific environment, they assume the corresponding role (dev-role, staging-role, prod-role), receiving temporary credentials scoped to that role's permissions. This enforces least privilege per environment. IAM groups apply cumulative permissions — adding a developer to multiple environment groups grants them all permissions simultaneously, not environment isolation. Permission boundaries limit the maximum permissions of an entity but do not create role-switching between environments. A single policy with conditions cannot isolate environment access the way separate roles can.

quiz.question: A company recently deployed Amazon Cognito User Pools for their customer-facing app. After a user successfully authenticates, what does the User Pool return to the client application?
options: Temporary AWS IAM credentials (access key, secret key, session token) for the authenticated user
A JWT token set (ID token, access token, refresh token) representing the authenticated user's identity
An API Gateway API key for the user to call backend services
An SAML assertion that the client uses to request AWS console access
quiz.explanation: Amazon Cognito User Pools return a set of JWT tokens upon successful authentication: an ID token (contains user attributes/claims), an access token (for authorizing API calls), and a refresh token (to get new tokens without re-authenticating). These are NOT AWS IAM credentials — to get AWS credentials, the JWT must be exchanged at a Cognito Identity Pool. An API Gateway API key is a different mechanism for API access control. A SAML assertion is issued by SAML identity providers — User Pools issue JWTs, not SAML assertions.

quiz.question: A financial services company is migrating its Windows Server workloads to AWS EC2. These workloads require Kerberos authentication, group policy enforcement, and LDAP access to an enterprise directory. They want to minimize management of domain controller infrastructure. Which AWS service BEST supports this migration?
options: AWS IAM Identity Center with Active Directory integration for Kerberos
AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD)
Amazon Cognito with a corporate LDAP custom authentication flow
AWS Secrets Manager storing service account credentials for AD-integrated applications
quiz.explanation: AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory provides a fully managed, actual Microsoft AD (not a simulation). It supports Kerberos authentication, LDAP, group policy, domain join, and all standard AD features. AWS manages the domain controllers — you do not need EC2 instances for them. IAM Identity Center provides SSO and can use Directory Service but is not itself a directory that supports Kerberos/LDAP for EC2 workloads. Cognito is designed for web/mobile app user authentication, not enterprise AD workload integration. Secrets Manager stores credentials but does not provide AD directory services.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is evaluating AWS Cognito for a new application. The business owner asks: 'What is the difference between User Pools and Identity Pools?' Which answer is MOST accurate?
options: User Pools store user profile data; Identity Pools store temporary AWS credential data
User Pools handle user authentication (sign-in) and return JWT tokens; Identity Pools grant users temporary AWS credentials (via STS) to access AWS services
User Pools are for mobile apps; Identity Pools are for web apps
User Pools manage external identity providers; Identity Pools manage internal app users
quiz.explanation: User Pools are authentication directories — they manage user sign-up, sign-in, MFA, and return JWT tokens. They answer 'who is this user?' Identity Pools are an authorization broker — they accept identity tokens (from User Pools, Google, Facebook, SAML) and exchange them for temporary AWS credentials via STS, answering 'what AWS resources can this user access?' Option A is an inaccurate description of both. Options C and D are incorrect — both User Pools and Identity Pools support web and mobile apps; Identity Pools support external identity providers (Google, Facebook) as well as User Pools.

lessons.title: AWS Machine Learning Services
lessons.overview: AWS provides a broad portfolio of managed ML services covering computer vision, NLP, speech, search, recommendations, forecasting, fraud detection, and developer tooling — each purpose-built so you don't need to train models from scratch. Understanding which service maps to which use case is the core exam skill for this lesson.
lessons.overviewVi: AWS cung cấp danh mục rộng các dịch vụ ML được quản lý hoàn toàn, bao gồm thị giác máy tính, xử lý ngôn ngữ tự nhiên, giọng nói, tìm kiếm, gợi ý và phát hiện gian lận — mỗi dịch vụ được xây dựng cho một mục đích cụ thể. Kỹ năng cốt lõi cho kỳ thi là biết dịch vụ nào phù hợp với tình huống nào.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: AWS ML Service Landscape
sections.contentHtml:
Rather than building and training ML models from scratch, AWS offers purpose-built managed ML services that let you call an API and get results. The foundational platform is Amazon SageMaker, and on top of it AWS layers domain-specific services organized by capability:

  • Computer Vision — Rekognition, Lookout for Vision, Panorama
  • Automated Data Extraction — Textract, Augmented AI, Comprehend
  • Language AI — Lex, Transcribe, Polly, Translate, Kendra, Personalize
  • Business Metrics — Forecast, Fraud Detector, Lookout for Metrics
  • DevOps/MLOps — DevOps Guru, CodeGuru Reviewer, CodeGuru Profiler, CodeWhisperer
Exam strategy: Match the scenario keyword to the service — image/video→Rekognition, OCR/scanned doc→Textract, speech-to-text→Transcribe, text-to-speech→Polly, chatbot→Lex.
sections.contentVi: Thay vì tự xây dựng và huấn luyện mô hình ML, AWS cung cấp các dịch vụ ML được quản lý hoàn toàn — chỉ cần gọi API là có kết quả. Nền tảng cốt lõi là Amazon SageMaker; phía trên là các dịch vụ chuyên biệt theo nhóm: Thị giác máy tính — Rekognition, Lookout for Vision, Panorama; Trích xuất dữ liệu tự động — Textract, Augmented AI, Comprehend; Ngôn ngữ AI — Lex, Transcribe, Polly, Translate, Kendra, Personalize; Chỉ số kinh doanh — Forecast, Fraud Detector, Lookout for Metrics; DevOps/MLOps — DevOps Guru, CodeGuru Reviewer, CodeGuru Profiler, CodeWhisperer. Chiến lược thi: khớp từ khóa tình huống với dịch vụ tương ứng.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Computer Vision and Data Extraction Services
sections.contentHtml:
Computer Vision

Amazon RekognitionObject, face, text, scene detection from images and videos; facial recognition; PPE detection; Custom Labels for custom components
Amazon Lookout for VisionDetect defects on industrial products at scale (manufacturing/factory); identifies dents, cracks, scratches using product images in S3
AWS PanoramaComputer vision at the edge for connected cameras

Automated Data Extraction

Amazon TextractOCR service; extracts text, forms, tables from scanned PDFs, documents, receipts, passports; query feature for natural language questions; batch upload via S3
Amazon Augmented AI (A2I)Human review workflows for ML output before next step; integrates with Rekognition and Textract
Amazon ComprehendNLP: extracts key phrases, sentiment, language, syntax, topics, PII from raw text — does NOT read scanned documents
Note: Comprehend needs raw text input — it cannot read scanned documents. Use Textract first if the source is a scanned/PDF document, then feed the extracted text to Comprehend.
sections.contentVi: Thị giác máy tính: Amazon Rekognition nhận diện đối tượng, khuôn mặt, văn bản, cảnh trong ảnh và video, có tính năng PPE detection và Custom Labels; Amazon Lookout for Vision phát hiện lỗi sản phẩm công nghiệp (dent, crack, scratch) qua ảnh trong S3; AWS Panorama dùng thị giác máy tính tại edge cho camera kết nối. Trích xuất dữ liệu tự động: Amazon Textract là dịch vụ OCR trích xuất text, form, bảng từ PDF scan, giấy tờ, hóa đơn, hộ chiếu, hỗ trợ query bằng ngôn ngữ tự nhiên; Amazon Augmented AI (A2I) tạo quy trình review thủ công cho ML output, tích hợp với Rekognition và Textract; Amazon Comprehend là NLP trích xuất cụm từ khóa, cảm xúc, ngôn ngữ, PII từ văn bản thô — KHÔNG đọc được tài liệu scan (cần dùng Textract trước).

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Language AI and Customer Experience Services
sections.contentHtml:
Language AI

Amazon LexBuild voice/text chatbots; self-service bots, virtual agents, IVR; reduces contact center costs
Amazon TranscribeSpeech-to-text; call transcripts, real-time transcription
Amazon PollyText-to-speech (opposite of Transcribe); lifelike speech in different voices; supports custom lexicon files and non-English text

Customer Experience

Amazon KendraIntelligent enterprise search across multiple data sources (S3, FSx, RDS, GitHub, Jira, Slack, SharePoint); NLP-powered — not just keyword matching
Amazon PersonalizePersonalized recommendations based on past behavior (like Netflix recommendation engine)
Amazon TranslateReal-time text translation; custom terminology; formality option; profanity masking
Key pair to remember: Transcribe = speech → text; Polly = text → speech. They are opposites.
sections.contentVi: Ngôn ngữ AI: Amazon Lex xây dựng chatbot giọng nói/văn bản, self-service bot, IVR giảm chi phí contact center; Amazon Transcribe chuyển giọng nói thành văn bản, dùng cho transcript cuộc gọi và phiên âm real-time; Amazon Polly chuyển văn bản thành giọng nói (ngược với Transcribe), hỗ trợ nhiều giọng đọc và file lexicon tùy chỉnh. Trải nghiệm khách hàng: Amazon Kendra tìm kiếm doanh nghiệp thông minh trên nhiều nguồn dữ liệu (S3, FSx, RDS, GitHub, Jira, Slack, SharePoint) dùng NLP, không chỉ khớp từ khóa; Amazon Personalize đề xuất cá nhân hóa dựa trên hành vi quá khứ (giống engine gợi ý của Netflix); Amazon Translate dịch văn bản real-time, hỗ trợ thuật ngữ tùy chỉnh, tùy chọn độ trang trọng và lọc ngôn từ không phù hợp.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Business Metrics and DevOps AI Services
sections.contentHtml:
Business Metrics

Amazon ForecastTime-series forecasting: predict sales, web traffic, inventory, revenue, cloud capacity, weather; uses Predictor ML model; built-in datasets (Weather Index, National Holidays)
Amazon Fraud DetectorIdentify fraudulent activity, fake reviews, spam account creation in near real-time; block by IP/email domain
Amazon Lookout for MetricsDetect anomalies in business metrics (sudden drop in sales, unexpected decline in acquisition); generates alerts

DevOps / MLOps

Amazon DevOps GuruDetects abnormal behavior in applications/AWS resources before downtime; analyzes RDS, Lambda, DynamoDB; supports cross-account
Amazon CodeGuru ReviewerIntelligent code review: scans for bad exception handling, insecure policy, hardcoded credentials; integrates with CI/CD
Amazon CodeGuru ProfilerCollects CPU data, analyzes runtime performance, identifies expensive code lines
Amazon CodeWhispererAI code generation from IDE comments; similar to GitHub Copilot
sections.contentVi: Chỉ số kinh doanh: Amazon Forecast dự báo chuỗi thời gian (doanh số, traffic web, tồn kho, doanh thu, sức chứa đám mây, thời tiết) dùng mô hình ML Predictor và dataset tích hợp sẵn; Amazon Fraud Detector nhận diện hoạt động gian lận, đánh giá giả, tạo tài khoản spam gần real-time, có thể chặn theo IP/tên miền email; Amazon Lookout for Metrics phát hiện bất thường trong chỉ số kinh doanh (doanh số giảm đột ngột, lượng người dùng mới giảm bất thường) và tạo cảnh báo. DevOps/MLOps: Amazon DevOps Guru phát hiện hành vi bất thường trong ứng dụng và tài nguyên AWS trước khi xảy ra downtime, hỗ trợ đa tài khoản; Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer review code thông minh, tìm xử lý exception kém, chính sách không an toàn, credential hardcode, tích hợp CI/CD; Amazon CodeGuru Profiler thu thập dữ liệu CPU, phân tích hiệu năng runtime, xác định dòng code tốn kém; Amazon CodeWhisperer sinh code từ comment trong IDE, tương tự GitHub Copilot.

sections.label: Deep Dive
sections.heading: Service Comparisons and Edge Cases
sections.contentHtml:
Several service pairs are commonly confused in the exam:

Textract vs ComprehendTextract extracts text FROM scanned documents (OCR); Comprehend analyzes raw text for NLP insights. Use Textract → then Comprehend when source is a scanned document.
Transcribe vs PollyTranscribe: audio → text. Polly: text → audio. Opposites.
Lex vs TranscribeLex builds conversational chatbots (intent-driven); Transcribe just converts speech to text with no conversational logic.
Rekognition vs Lookout for VisionRekognition is general-purpose image/video AI; Lookout for Vision is specialized for industrial defect detection in manufacturing.
Kendra vs ElasticsearchKendra is enterprise intelligent search with NLP across many data sources; Elasticsearch (OpenSearch) is a managed search/analytics engine for custom full-text search implementations.
Forecast vs Lookout for MetricsForecast predicts future values; Lookout for Metrics detects anomalies in current/historical data.
sections.contentVi: Các cặp dịch vụ hay bị nhầm lẫn trong kỳ thi: Textract vs Comprehend — Textract trích xuất text TỪ tài liệu scan (OCR); Comprehend phân tích text thô bằng NLP. Khi nguồn là tài liệu scan, dùng Textract trước rồi mới dùng Comprehend. Transcribe vs Polly — Transcribe: âm thanh → text; Polly: text → âm thanh; đây là hai chiều ngược nhau. Lex vs Transcribe — Lex xây chatbot hội thoại theo intent; Transcribe chỉ chuyển giọng nói thành text không có logic hội thoại. Rekognition vs Lookout for Vision — Rekognition là AI ảnh/video đa năng; Lookout for Vision chuyên phát hiện lỗi sản phẩm công nghiệp trong nhà máy. Kendra vs Elasticsearch — Kendra là tìm kiếm doanh nghiệp thông minh NLP trên nhiều nguồn dữ liệu; Elasticsearch là engine tìm kiếm/phân tích tùy chỉnh. Forecast vs Lookout for Metrics — Forecast dự đoán giá trị tương lai; Lookout for Metrics phát hiện bất thường trong dữ liệu hiện tại/lịch sử.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Trigger Words and Service Mapping
sections.contentHtml:
Map these scenario keywords to the correct AWS ML service:

  • Scanned PDF / OCR / forms / receipts / passports → Amazon Textract
  • Image / video analysis / facial recognition / PPE → Amazon Rekognition
  • Industrial defect detection / manufacturing quality → Amazon Lookout for Vision
  • Speech-to-text / call transcription → Amazon Transcribe
  • Text-to-speech / lifelike voice → Amazon Polly
  • Chatbot / IVR / virtual agent / contact center → Amazon Lex
  • Sentiment / key phrases / PII from text → Amazon Comprehend
  • Enterprise search / multiple data sources / NLP search → Amazon Kendra
  • Personalized recommendations → Amazon Personalize
  • Sales / inventory / time-series forecasting → Amazon Forecast
  • Fraud / fake accounts / online abuse → Amazon Fraud Detector
  • Business metric anomalies → Amazon Lookout for Metrics
  • Proactive application health / before downtime → Amazon DevOps Guru
  • Automated code review / security scan in CI/CD → Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer
  • AI code generation from IDE → Amazon CodeWhisperer
Tip: Amazon SageMaker appears in questions about building/training/deploying custom ML models — it's the platform when you need to train your own model rather than call a pre-built service.
sections.contentVi: Khớp từ khóa tình huống với dịch vụ AWS ML: PDF scan/OCR/form/hóa đơn/hộ chiếu → Textract; phân tích ảnh/video/nhận diện khuôn mặt/PPE → Rekognition; phát hiện lỗi công nghiệp/kiểm soát chất lượng sản xuất → Lookout for Vision; chuyển giọng nói thành text/transcript cuộc gọi → Transcribe; chuyển text thành giọng nói → Polly; chatbot/IVR/virtual agent/contact center → Lex; cảm xúc/cụm từ khóa/PII từ text → Comprehend; tìm kiếm doanh nghiệp/nhiều nguồn dữ liệu → Kendra; gợi ý cá nhân hóa → Personalize; dự báo doanh số/tồn kho/chuỗi thời gian → Forecast; gian lận/tài khoản giả/spam → Fraud Detector; bất thường chỉ số kinh doanh → Lookout for Metrics; sức khỏe ứng dụng chủ động/trước downtime → DevOps Guru; review code tự động/scan bảo mật CI/CD → CodeGuru Reviewer; sinh code từ IDE → CodeWhisperer. SageMaker xuất hiện khi cần xây dựng/huấn luyện/deploy mô hình ML tùy chỉnh.

summary: Amazon SageMaker is the full ML platform for building/training/deploying custom models; all other services are purpose-built APIs that require no ML expertise
Key OCR/NLP chain: Textract (scan→text) → Comprehend (text→NLP insights); Comprehend cannot read scanned documents directly
Speech pair: Transcribe (audio→text) and Polly (text→audio) are opposites; Lex builds full conversational chatbots on top of speech
Kendra provides NLP-powered enterprise search across many data sources; Personalize delivers Netflix-style recommendation engines
DevOps Guru detects application anomalies proactively; CodeGuru Reviewer automates security code review in CI/CD pipelines
summaryVi: Amazon SageMaker là nền tảng ML đầy đủ để xây dựng/huấn luyện/deploy mô hình tùy chỉnh; các dịch vụ khác là API chuyên dụng không cần kiến thức ML
Chuỗi OCR/NLP quan trọng: Textract (scan→text) → Comprehend (text→NLP); Comprehend không đọc được tài liệu scan trực tiếp
Cặp giọng nói: Transcribe (âm thanh→text) và Polly (text→âm thanh) là ngược nhau; Lex xây chatbot hội thoại đầy đủ
Kendra cung cấp tìm kiếm doanh nghiệp NLP trên nhiều nguồn dữ liệu; Personalize cung cấp engine gợi ý theo phong cách Netflix
DevOps Guru phát hiện bất thường ứng dụng chủ động; CodeGuru Reviewer tự động hóa review code bảo mật trong CI/CD

quiz.question: A retail company receives thousands of invoices and receipts in PDF format each day. They need to automatically extract line items, totals, and vendor information to populate their accounting system. Which AWS service is MOST appropriate?
options: Amazon Comprehend
Amazon Rekognition
Amazon Textract
Amazon Kendra
quiz.explanation: Amazon Textract is an OCR service specifically designed to extract text, forms, and tables from scanned documents including PDFs, receipts, and invoices. Comprehend analyzes raw text for NLP insights but cannot read scanned documents. Rekognition is for image/video analysis, not document OCR. Kendra is an enterprise search service, not a document extraction service.

quiz.question: A manufacturing company wants to automatically inspect product images on an assembly line and detect surface defects such as dents, cracks, and scratches before products are shipped. The images are stored in Amazon S3. Which AWS service BEST meets this requirement?
options: Amazon Rekognition Custom Labels
Amazon Lookout for Vision
Amazon SageMaker Canvas
AWS Panorama
quiz.explanation: Amazon Lookout for Vision is purpose-built for industrial defect detection at scale — it uses product images stored in S3 to detect dents, cracks, and scratches in manufacturing environments. Rekognition Custom Labels can train custom models but is not specialized for industrial quality control. SageMaker Canvas is a no-code ML model building tool. Panorama is for edge-based computer vision with cameras.

quiz.question: A contact center wants to automatically transcribe all customer calls into text and then analyze the transcripts to detect customer sentiment and extract common complaint topics. Which combination of services is MOST cost-effective?
options: Amazon Polly and Amazon Comprehend
Amazon Transcribe and Amazon Comprehend
Amazon Lex and Amazon Kendra
Amazon Transcribe and Amazon Rekognition
quiz.explanation: Amazon Transcribe converts speech (call recordings) to text, and Amazon Comprehend then analyzes that raw text to extract sentiment, key phrases, and topics. Polly is text-to-speech (the opposite direction). Lex builds chatbots, not transcription. Kendra is enterprise search. Rekognition analyzes images/videos, not text.

quiz.question: A company is building a customer service platform and wants to create a voice-driven IVR (Interactive Voice Response) system that understands customer intents and routes calls without human agents. Which AWS service should they use?
options: Amazon Transcribe
Amazon Polly
Amazon Lex
Amazon Comprehend
quiz.explanation: Amazon Lex is designed to build conversational chatbots and virtual agents, including IVR systems that understand intents and route interactions. Transcribe only converts speech to text without conversational logic. Polly converts text to speech. Comprehend analyzes text for NLP insights but does not build conversational flows.

quiz.question: A media company wants to convert their library of written articles into audio format for a podcast service, supporting multiple voices and languages including French and Spanish. Which AWS service is MOST appropriate?
options: Amazon Transcribe
Amazon Lex
Amazon Translate
Amazon Polly
quiz.explanation: Amazon Polly converts text to lifelike speech in different voices and supports non-English text including French and Spanish — exactly the use case described. Transcribe is the reverse direction (audio to text). Lex builds conversational bots, not text-to-speech. Translate converts text between languages but does not produce audio.

quiz.question: An enterprise has documents across SharePoint, GitHub, Jira, S3, and RDS. Employees need to search across all these sources using natural language questions like 'What is the vacation policy?' instead of exact keyword searches. Which AWS service BEST meets this requirement?
options: Amazon Elasticsearch Service
Amazon Kendra
Amazon Comprehend
Amazon CloudSearch
quiz.explanation: Amazon Kendra is an intelligent enterprise search service that uses NLP to understand natural language questions and searches across multiple data sources including SharePoint, GitHub, Jira, S3, and RDS. Elasticsearch is a managed search engine for custom implementations, not a pre-built enterprise search with NLP. Comprehend analyzes text for NLP insights but is not a search service. CloudSearch is for implementing search within applications, not cross-source enterprise search.

quiz.question: A streaming service wants to implement a recommendation engine that suggests movies based on each user's viewing history and behavior, similar to how Netflix recommends content. Which AWS service provides this capability with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Amazon Kendra
Amazon Forecast
Amazon Personalize
Amazon SageMaker
quiz.explanation: Amazon Personalize is a fully managed service that provides personalized recommendations based on past user behavior — it's specifically designed for this Netflix-style recommendation use case with least operational overhead. Kendra is for enterprise document search. Forecast does time-series predictions for business metrics. SageMaker could be used but requires building and training a custom model, which has much higher operational overhead.

quiz.question: A retail chain wants to predict their holiday inventory needs for the next 6 months using historical sales data, incorporating seasonal trends and national holidays. Which AWS service is designed for this use case?
options: Amazon Lookout for Metrics
Amazon Fraud Detector
Amazon Forecast
Amazon Comprehend
quiz.explanation: Amazon Forecast is a time-series forecasting service designed for predicting future values like sales and inventory. It includes built-in datasets for Weather Index and National Holidays, making it ideal for incorporating seasonal factors. Lookout for Metrics detects anomalies in existing data — it doesn't predict future values. Fraud Detector identifies fraudulent activity. Comprehend is for NLP text analysis.

quiz.question: An e-commerce platform notices a sudden unexplained 40% drop in conversion rates and wants to automatically detect such anomalies in their business metrics and receive alerts. Which AWS service BEST addresses this?
options: Amazon Forecast
Amazon Lookout for Metrics
Amazon DevOps Guru
Amazon CloudWatch Anomaly Detection
quiz.explanation: Amazon Lookout for Metrics is specifically designed to detect anomalies in business metrics such as sudden drops in sales, conversion rates, or unexpected declines in user acquisition, and it generates alerts. Forecast predicts future values rather than detecting current anomalies. DevOps Guru monitors application/infrastructure health, not business metrics. CloudWatch Anomaly Detection monitors operational metrics, not business KPIs.

quiz.question: A financial services company processes loan applications and wants to detect potentially fraudulent applications and fake account creation attempts in near real-time, blocking requests from suspicious IP addresses and email domains. Which AWS service MOST directly addresses this?
options: Amazon Lookout for Metrics
AWS WAF with rate limiting
Amazon Fraud Detector
Amazon Macie
quiz.explanation: Amazon Fraud Detector is purpose-built to identify fraudulent activity, fake account creation, and spam in near real-time, with the ability to block by IP or email domain. Lookout for Metrics detects anomalies in business metrics. WAF with rate limiting protects against web attacks but is not an ML-based fraud detection service. Macie discovers and protects sensitive data — it does not detect fraud.

quiz.question: A DevOps team wants to automatically detect potential issues in their AWS environment — such as high RDS DB load, unusual Lambda invocation patterns, and over-provisioned DynamoDB capacity — before they cause downtime. Which AWS service provides this proactive monitoring?
options: Amazon CodeGuru Profiler
AWS Config
Amazon DevOps Guru
AWS Trusted Advisor
quiz.explanation: Amazon DevOps Guru uses ML to detect abnormal behavior in applications and AWS resources (including RDS, Lambda, and DynamoDB) before downtime occurs. It supports cross-account analysis. CodeGuru Profiler analyzes application runtime performance (CPU/code-level), not infrastructure health. AWS Config tracks configuration changes but does not proactively detect anomalies. Trusted Advisor gives best-practice recommendations but is not a real-time anomaly detection service.

quiz.question: A software development team wants to automatically scan their code for hardcoded credentials, insecure IAM policies, and poor exception handling as part of their CI/CD pipeline. Which AWS service provides this capability?
options: Amazon CodeGuru Profiler
Amazon CodeWhisperer
AWS CodePipeline with manual approval
Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer
quiz.explanation: Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer uses ML to provide intelligent code review recommendations, specifically scanning for bad exception handling, insecure policies, and hardcoded credentials. It integrates with CI/CD pipelines. CodeGuru Profiler monitors runtime CPU performance, not code security. CodeWhisperer generates code from comments — it does not review existing code for security issues. CodePipeline with manual approval requires human reviewers, not automated ML scanning.

quiz.question: A legal firm receives scanned court documents in PDF format and wants to analyze them to extract entity names, detect legal sentiment, and identify personally identifiable information. What is the CORRECT sequence of AWS services to use?
options: Amazon Comprehend → Amazon Textract
Amazon Rekognition → Amazon Comprehend
Amazon Textract → Amazon Comprehend
Amazon Kendra → Amazon Comprehend
quiz.explanation: Scanned PDFs require OCR first: Amazon Textract extracts raw text from the scanned documents, then Amazon Comprehend analyzes that raw text to extract entities, sentiment, and PII. Comprehend cannot process scanned documents directly — it requires raw text input. Rekognition is for image/video analysis, not document OCR. Kendra is a search service, not an OCR or NLP extraction pipeline.

quiz.question: A global company is building a multi-language customer support platform. They need to automatically transcribe English phone calls into text AND convert support documentation into audio for hearing-impaired customers. (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon Lex for call transcription
Amazon Transcribe for converting call audio to text
Amazon Polly for converting documentation text to audio
Amazon Comprehend for audio conversion
Amazon Translate for transcription
quiz.explanation: Amazon Transcribe converts speech (phone calls) to text, and Amazon Polly converts text (documentation) to lifelike audio. Lex builds conversational chatbots — it does not transcribe calls. Comprehend performs NLP text analysis, not audio conversion. Translate converts text between languages but does not produce audio.

quiz.question: A developer is writing backend code in their IDE and wants AI-powered suggestions that generate entire functions from comment descriptions, similar to GitHub Copilot. Which AWS service provides this?
options: Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer
Amazon CodeGuru Profiler
Amazon CodeWhisperer
Amazon DevOps Guru
quiz.explanation: Amazon CodeWhisperer is an AI code generation service that generates code suggestions from natural language comments in the IDE, functioning similarly to GitHub Copilot.
