CodeGuru Reviewer scans existing code for issues. CodeGuru Profiler analyzes runtime CPU performance. DevOps Guru monitors application health — none of these generate code from IDE comments.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is designing an ML-based customer analytics system. The system must extract structured data from scanned customer feedback forms AND detect customer sentiment from the extracted text. Which AWS services should be used? (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon Rekognition
Amazon Textract
Amazon Comprehend
Amazon Forecast
Amazon Kendra
quiz.explanation: Amazon Textract extracts structured data (text, forms, tables) from scanned documents, and Amazon Comprehend then analyzes the raw text to detect sentiment. Rekognition is for image/video analysis, not form OCR. Forecast is for time-series predictions, not sentiment analysis. Kendra is an enterprise search service.

quiz.question: A quality assurance team at an automotive manufacturer wants a human expert to review ML predictions about paint defects before the system takes automated action. Which AWS service enables this human-in-the-loop review workflow?
options: Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
Amazon Augmented AI (A2I)
Amazon Rekognition Custom Labels
AWS Step Functions
quiz.explanation: Amazon Augmented AI (A2I) is designed specifically for human review workflows for ML use cases — it routes ML outputs for human review before the next step in the workflow, integrating natively with Rekognition and Textract. SageMaker Ground Truth is for labeling training data, not for reviewing live predictions. Rekognition Custom Labels trains models but does not add a human review step. Step Functions orchestrates workflows but has no built-in ML human review capability.

lessons.title: AWS Transfer and Migration Services
lessons.overview: AWS provides both online and offline data transfer services plus a suite of migration tools for moving workloads, databases, and entire data centers to the cloud. Choosing the right service depends on data volume, connectivity, migration type, and whether ongoing replication is needed.
lessons.overviewVi: AWS cung cấp cả dịch vụ truyền dữ liệu trực tuyến lẫn ngoại tuyến, cùng bộ công cụ di chuyển workload, cơ sở dữ liệu và toàn bộ data center lên đám mây. Việc chọn dịch vụ phù hợp phụ thuộc vào khối lượng dữ liệu, kết nối mạng, loại migration và nhu cầu sao chép liên tục.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Transfer vs Migration: When to Use What
sections.contentHtml:
AWS migration and transfer services fall into two broad categories:

Online TransferData moves over the network (internet or Direct Connect): AWS DataSync, AWS Transfer Family
Offline TransferPhysical device shipped to/from AWS: Snow Family (Snowcone, Snowball, Snowmobile)
Migration ServicesTools for planning and executing moves of servers, databases, and applications: Application Discovery, DMS, SMS, Migration Hub, Migration Evaluator
Exam decision rule: If the question mentions limited bandwidth, large data volume, or offline → Snow Family. If it mentions ongoing replication or migration planning → DataSync, DMS, or Migration Hub.
sections.contentVi: Dịch vụ migration và transfer của AWS chia thành hai nhóm chính: Truyền trực tuyến — dữ liệu di chuyển qua mạng (internet hoặc Direct Connect): AWS DataSync, AWS Transfer Family; Truyền ngoại tuyến — thiết bị vật lý vận chuyển đến/từ AWS: Snow Family (Snowcone, Snowball, Snowmobile); Dịch vụ Migration — công cụ lập kế hoạch và thực hiện di chuyển server, cơ sở dữ liệu, ứng dụng: Application Discovery, DMS, SMS, Migration Hub, Migration Evaluator. Quy tắc thi: nếu câu hỏi đề cập băng thông hạn chế, khối lượng lớn hoặc offline → Snow Family; nếu đề cập sao chép liên tục hoặc lập kế hoạch migration → DataSync, DMS hoặc Migration Hub.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Online Transfer: DataSync and Transfer Family
sections.contentHtml:
AWS DataSync

Automates and accelerates online data replication between on-premises storage and AWS storage services. Uses a DataSync agent (a VM installed on-premises) and transfers over internet or Direct Connect.

  • Supports: SMB/NFS file servers, self-managed object storage, Snowcone, S3, EFS, FSx for Windows
  • Primary use case: migrate data to AWS
  • For ongoing on-premises integration after migration → use Storage Gateway instead

AWS Transfer Family

Managed file transfer service to/from Amazon S3 using standard protocols:

ProtocolsSFTP, FTPS, FTP — separate service endpoint per protocol
AuthIntegrates with existing authentication systems
DNSIntegrates with Route 53 for custom domain names
DataSync vs Storage Gateway: DataSync = one-time or scheduled migration to AWS; Storage Gateway = ongoing hybrid integration where on-prem applications continue to need access to data in AWS.
sections.contentVi: AWS DataSync tự động hóa và tăng tốc sao chép dữ liệu trực tuyến giữa lưu trữ on-premises và AWS. Dùng DataSync agent (VM cài trên on-prem), truyền qua internet hoặc Direct Connect. Hỗ trợ: SMB/NFS file server, object storage tự quản lý, Snowcone, S3, EFS, FSx for Windows. Ứng dụng chính: di chuyển dữ liệu lên AWS. Nếu cần tích hợp on-prem liên tục sau migration → dùng Storage Gateway. AWS Transfer Family là dịch vụ truyền file đến/từ S3 dùng giao thức chuẩn SFTP/FTPS/FTP, mỗi giao thức là endpoint riêng biệt, tích hợp hệ thống xác thực hiện có và Route 53 cho tên miền tùy chỉnh. Lưu ý: DataSync cho migration một lần hoặc theo lịch; Storage Gateway cho tích hợp hybrid liên tục khi ứng dụng on-prem vẫn cần truy cập dữ liệu trên AWS.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Offline Transfer: AWS Snow Family
sections.contentHtml:
When network bandwidth is insufficient for large data transfers, AWS ships physical devices:

AWS SnowconeSmallest device: ~4.5 lbs / ~2 kg; 8 TB usable storage; load via NFS mount; ship back → auto upload to S3
AWS SnowballLarger: ~50 lbs / ~22 kg; 80 TB usable; petabyte-scale migrations; for limited network scenarios
AWS Snowmobile45-foot shipping container on a semi-trailer truck; up to 100 petabytes; exabyte-scale migration; complete data center moves
Size selector: Small amounts → Snowcone; petabyte-scale → Snowball; exabyte-scale / whole data center → Snowmobile.
sections.contentVi: Khi băng thông mạng không đủ cho lượng dữ liệu lớn, AWS giao thiết bị vật lý: AWS Snowcone là thiết bị nhỏ nhất ~2 kg, 8 TB dung lượng sử dụng, nạp dữ liệu qua NFS, gửi lại AWS → tự động upload lên S3; AWS Snowball lớn hơn ~22 kg, 80 TB, cho migration quy mô petabyte trong điều kiện mạng hạn chế; AWS Snowmobile là container 45 feet trên xe tải bán kéo, tối đa 100 petabytes, quy mô exabyte, dùng cho di chuyển toàn bộ data center. Chọn thiết bị: lượng nhỏ → Snowcone; petabyte → Snowball; exabyte / cả data center → Snowmobile.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Migration Planning and Execution Services
sections.contentHtml:
Discovery and Planning

AWS Application Discovery ServiceGathers on-premises data center info for migration planning; Discovery Agent captures: system config, performance, running processes, network connections between systems
Migration EvaluatorMigration assessment; builds business case; creates statistical model of compute patterns; shows cost analysis and over-provisioning opportunities
AWS Migration HubSingle dashboard to track migration status across tools; does NOT execute migrations — visibility only

Execution

AWS DMSDatabase Migration Service; source DB stays operational during migration (minimal downtime); heterogeneous migrations need AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) to convert schema; supports continuous replication
AWS SMSServer Migration Service; agentless; migrates on-premises VMware workloads to AWS; uses SMS connector in VMware vCenter; automates/schedules incremental replications
Migration Hub vs DMS/SMS: Migration Hub provides visibility and tracking; DMS and SMS actually execute the migration.
sections.contentVi: Khám phá và lập kế hoạch: AWS Application Discovery Service thu thập thông tin data center on-prem cho kế hoạch migration, Discovery Agent ghi lại cấu hình hệ thống, hiệu năng, tiến trình đang chạy và kết nối mạng giữa các hệ thống; Migration Evaluator đánh giá migration, xây dựng business case, tạo mô hình thống kê về mẫu compute, phân tích chi phí và cơ hội tối ưu; AWS Migration Hub là bảng điều khiển duy nhất theo dõi trạng thái migration — KHÔNG thực hiện migration, chỉ hiển thị trạng thái. Thực thi: AWS DMS (Database Migration Service) di chuyển cơ sở dữ liệu, DB nguồn vẫn hoạt động trong quá trình (ít downtime), migration dị chất cần Schema Conversion Tool (SCT), hỗ trợ sao chép liên tục; AWS SMS (Server Migration Service) không cần agent, di chuyển workload VMware on-prem lên AWS qua SMS connector trong vCenter, tự động hóa sao chép tăng dần.

sections.label: Deep Dive
sections.heading: DMS: Homogeneous vs Heterogeneous Migration
sections.contentHtml:
AWS DMS supports two types of database migration:

HomogeneousSame DB engine (MySQL → MySQL, Oracle → Oracle); DMS handles migration directly; no schema conversion needed
HeterogeneousDifferent DB engines (PostgreSQL → MySQL, Oracle → Aurora, Cassandra → DynamoDB); requires AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) first to convert schema, then DMS migrates data

Key DMS features for the exam:

  • Source database stays fully operational during migration — minimal to zero downtime
  • Supports continuous replication for ongoing synchronization
  • Supports one-time migration or ongoing replication
Exam trigger: If the question mentions "different database engines" or schema incompatibility → DMS + SCT. If same engine → DMS alone.
sections.contentVi: AWS DMS hỗ trợ hai loại migration cơ sở dữ liệu: Đồng chất (Homogeneous) — cùng engine DB (MySQL→MySQL, Oracle→Oracle); DMS xử lý trực tiếp, không cần chuyển đổi schema. Dị chất (Heterogeneous) — khác engine DB (PostgreSQL→MySQL, Oracle→Aurora, Cassandra→DynamoDB); cần Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) chuyển đổi schema trước, sau đó DMS di chuyển dữ liệu. Tính năng quan trọng DMS trong thi: DB nguồn vẫn hoạt động đầy đủ trong migration — ít hoặc không có downtime; hỗ trợ sao chép liên tục; hỗ trợ cả migration một lần và sao chép liên tục. Gợi ý thi: câu hỏi đề cập 'engine DB khác nhau' hoặc không tương thích schema → DMS + SCT; cùng engine → chỉ DMS.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Service Selection Decision Tree
sections.contentHtml:
Use this decision logic for transfer/migration questions:

  • Large data, limited/no internet bandwidth → Snow Family (size: TB→Snowcone, petabytes→Snowball, exabytes→Snowmobile)
  • Migrate data online to AWS (one-time or scheduled) → AWS DataSync with DataSync agent
  • Ongoing hybrid on-premises + AWS file access → AWS Storage Gateway (NOT DataSync)
  • File transfer to S3 via SFTP/FTP → AWS Transfer Family
  • Migrate database with minimal downtime → AWS DMS; add SCT for different engines
  • Migrate VMware VMs to AWS (agentless) → AWS SMS with SMS connector in vCenter
  • Discover on-premises environment for migration planning → Application Discovery Service
  • Track migration progress across tools → AWS Migration Hub (tracking only, not execution)
  • Build business case / cost analysis for migration → Migration Evaluator
Common trap: Migration Hub does NOT execute migrations — it only tracks them. DMS and SMS actually move data/workloads.
sections.contentVi: Logic chọn dịch vụ cho câu hỏi transfer/migration: Dữ liệu lớn, băng thông hạn chế/không có → Snow Family (TB→Snowcone, petabytes→Snowball, exabytes→Snowmobile); Di chuyển dữ liệu trực tuyến lên AWS (một lần hoặc theo lịch) → AWS DataSync với DataSync agent; Tích hợp hybrid liên tục on-prem + AWS → Storage Gateway (KHÔNG phải DataSync); Truyền file lên S3 qua SFTP/FTP → Transfer Family; Di chuyển DB với ít downtime → AWS DMS, thêm SCT nếu khác engine; Di chuyển VMware VMs lên AWS (không cần agent) → AWS SMS với SMS connector trong vCenter; Khám phá môi trường on-prem cho kế hoạch → Application Discovery Service; Theo dõi tiến độ migration → Migration Hub (chỉ theo dõi, không thực hiện); Xây dựng business case/phân tích chi phí → Migration Evaluator. Bẫy thường gặp: Migration Hub KHÔNG thực hiện migration — chỉ theo dõi.

summary: Snow Family covers offline transfer: Snowcone (8 TB, portable), Snowball (80 TB, petabyte-scale), Snowmobile (100 PB, exabyte-scale / full data center)
DataSync uses an on-premises agent to migrate data online to AWS; use Storage Gateway instead when ongoing hybrid access is needed after migration
AWS Transfer Family enables SFTP/FTPS/FTP file transfers directly to S3 with existing auth system integration
DMS migrates databases with minimal downtime (source stays live); heterogeneous migrations (different engines) require Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) first
Migration Hub tracks migration status across tools but does NOT execute migrations; Application Discovery Service discovers on-prem systems; Migration Evaluator builds the business case
summaryVi: Snow Family: Snowcone (8 TB, di động), Snowball (80 TB, quy mô petabyte), Snowmobile (100 PB, quy mô exabyte / toàn bộ data center)
DataSync dùng agent on-prem để di chuyển dữ liệu trực tuyến lên AWS; dùng Storage Gateway khi cần truy cập hybrid liên tục sau migration
Transfer Family cho phép truyền file SFTP/FTPS/FTP trực tiếp lên S3 tích hợp hệ thống xác thực hiện có
DMS di chuyển cơ sở dữ liệu với ít downtime (DB nguồn vẫn chạy); migration dị chất (khác engine) cần Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) trước
Migration Hub theo dõi trạng thái migration nhưng KHÔNG thực hiện; Application Discovery Service khám phá on-prem; Migration Evaluator xây business case

quiz.question: A company needs to transfer 500 TB of archival data from their on-premises data center to Amazon S3. Their internet connection is 100 Mbps and is shared with production traffic. Transferring online would take months. Which solution provides the MOST cost-effective and FASTEST transfer?
options: AWS DataSync over AWS Direct Connect
AWS Snowball devices
AWS Transfer Family with SFTP
S3 multipart upload over the internet
quiz.explanation: AWS Snowball (80 TB usable) is the correct offline transfer solution for petabyte-scale data when network bandwidth is insufficient. Multiple Snowball devices can be used for 500 TB. DataSync over Direct Connect would still be slow without a dedicated high-bandwidth connection and would not solve the bandwidth contention issue. Transfer Family uses SFTP to S3 but is still an online transfer. S3 multipart upload does not resolve the bandwidth constraint.

quiz.question: A media production company needs to move 10 exabytes of raw video footage from their physical studios to AWS as part of a complete data center decommission. Which AWS service is designed for this scale?
options: Multiple AWS Snowball devices in parallel
AWS Snowmobile
AWS DataSync with multiple agents
AWS Direct Connect with 100 Gbps links
quiz.explanation: AWS Snowmobile is a 45-foot shipping container truck capable of transferring up to 100 petabytes per truck and is designed for exabyte-scale data center migrations. Multiple Snowball devices at 80 TB each would require hundreds of devices and complex logistics. DataSync requires network bandwidth that makes exabyte-scale impractical. Direct Connect at 100 Gbps would still take years to transfer exabytes.

quiz.question: A field engineer needs to collect IoT sensor data from a remote oil rig with no internet connectivity and ship it to AWS. The data volume is approximately 5 TB. Which Snow Family device is MOST appropriate?
options: AWS Snowmobile
AWS Snowball
AWS Snowcone
AWS DataSync agent on an EC2 instance
quiz.explanation: AWS Snowcone is the smallest and most portable Snow Family device at ~2 kg with 8 TB usable storage, making it ideal for remote field locations with no internet connectivity. 5 TB fits within Snowcone's 8 TB capacity. Snowmobile is for exabyte-scale moves and is impractical for a remote oil rig. Snowball at 80 TB is oversized for 5 TB. DataSync requires network connectivity which is not available.

quiz.question: A company is migrating their on-premises NFS file server data to Amazon EFS. They want to automate and accelerate the transfer using their existing Direct Connect connection, with an agent installed on-premises. Which service should they use?
options: AWS Transfer Family
AWS Storage Gateway
AWS DataSync
AWS Snowball Edge
quiz.explanation: AWS DataSync is designed to automate and accelerate online data migration from on-premises NFS file servers to AWS storage (including EFS) using a DataSync agent installed on-premises, over Direct Connect or internet. Transfer Family enables SFTP/FTP access to S3, not NFS-to-EFS migration. Storage Gateway provides ongoing hybrid access, not one-time migration. Snowball is an offline physical device, not an online agent-based transfer.

quiz.question: After completing a data migration to AWS using DataSync, a company's on-premises applications still need to read and write files to the same data that is now stored in Amazon S3, with low-latency local access. Which service should replace DataSync for the ongoing integration?
options: Continue using DataSync with continuous replication
AWS Transfer Family with SFTP
AWS Storage Gateway
AWS Direct Connect with S3 VPC endpoint
quiz.explanation: AWS Storage Gateway is designed for ongoing hybrid integration where on-premises applications need continuous access to data stored in AWS. DataSync is optimized for one-time or scheduled migrations, not for ongoing low-latency hybrid file access. Transfer Family requires SFTP clients, not transparent file system access for applications. Direct Connect with S3 VPC endpoint provides connectivity but not a file gateway interface for on-premises applications.

quiz.question: A financial institution uses SFTP to exchange files with trading partners. They are migrating their SFTP server from on-premises to AWS while keeping the same hostnames, usernames, and authentication workflow. Which service BEST meets this requirement?
options: Amazon S3 with presigned URLs
AWS DataSync over SFTP
AWS Transfer Family
AWS Snowball with SFTP endpoint
quiz.explanation: AWS Transfer Family provides a fully managed SFTP service that stores files in S3, integrates with existing authentication systems, and works with Route 53 for maintaining existing hostnames. This allows trading partners to continue using the same SFTP workflow transparently. S3 presigned URLs are not SFTP-compatible. DataSync does not expose an SFTP server endpoint. Snowball is a physical offline transfer device.

quiz.question: A company is migrating their Oracle database to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL. The migration must keep the source Oracle database fully operational during the transition to minimize downtime. The schemas are incompatible between Oracle and PostgreSQL. Which combination of AWS services should be used?
options: AWS SMS to replicate the Oracle server, then export/import
AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) followed by AWS DMS
AWS DMS alone with Oracle-to-Aurora native connector
AWS DataSync to transfer database files, then restore on Aurora
quiz.explanation: This is a heterogeneous migration (Oracle → Aurora PostgreSQL = different engines). AWS SCT must first convert the schema, then AWS DMS migrates the data while the source Oracle database remains operational. DMS alone cannot handle schema conversion between incompatible engines. SMS migrates server workloads, not databases. DataSync transfers files but cannot migrate live database transactions while keeping the source operational.

quiz.question: A DevOps team is migrating 200 VMware virtual machines from their on-premises vCenter environment to AWS. They need an agentless solution that can schedule and automate incremental replications. Which service BEST fits?
options: AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)
AWS Server Migration Service (SMS) with SMS connector in vCenter
AWS DMS with continuous replication
AWS DataSync with VMware agent
quiz.explanation: AWS Server Migration Service (SMS) is agentless and uses an SMS connector installed in VMware vCenter to automate and schedule incremental replications of VMs to AWS. DMS is for database migration, not VM workloads. DataSync transfers file system data, not VM snapshots. Application Migration Service is also valid for VM migration but SMS specifically targets VMware vCenter environments with the connector approach described.

quiz.question: A large enterprise wants to plan a migration of their on-premises data center to AWS. They need to first understand all running processes, system performance metrics, and network dependencies between their 500 servers before creating a migration plan. Which AWS service addresses this discovery phase?
options: AWS Migration Hub
AWS Config with on-premises integration
AWS Application Discovery Service
Migration Evaluator
quiz.explanation: AWS Application Discovery Service uses a Discovery Agent installed on on-premises servers to collect system configuration, performance data, running processes, and network connections between systems — exactly the information needed for migration planning. Migration Hub tracks migration progress but does not discover/collect server data. AWS Config monitors AWS resource configurations, not on-premises systems. Migration Evaluator builds a financial business case, not a technical dependency map.

quiz.question: A CTO wants to present a business case to the board for migrating to AWS, including projected cost savings, rightsizing opportunities, and an analysis of current over-provisioning. Which AWS tool is designed specifically for this purpose?
options: AWS Migration Hub
AWS Trusted Advisor
AWS Application Discovery Service
Migration Evaluator
quiz.explanation: Migration Evaluator (formerly TSO Logic) is designed specifically to build a migration business case — it creates statistical models of existing compute patterns, identifies over-provisioning, and generates cost analysis comparing on-premises to AWS. Migration Hub tracks migration execution status. Trusted Advisor gives best-practice recommendations for existing AWS resources, not pre-migration cost analysis. Application Discovery Service maps technical dependencies, not financial business cases.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is managing a large-scale migration involving multiple tools: DMS for databases, SMS for servers, and Snowball for bulk data. The project manager wants a single dashboard to see the overall migration progress without switching between tools. Which service provides this?
options: AWS Systems Manager
AWS Migration Hub
Amazon CloudWatch with custom dashboards
AWS Control Tower
quiz.explanation: AWS Migration Hub provides a single place to track migration status across multiple AWS migration tools including DMS, SMS, and others. It aggregates progress visibility without executing the migrations itself. Systems Manager manages operational tasks on running instances, not migration tracking. CloudWatch could be customized but is not a migration-specific tracking service. Control Tower sets up multi-account governance, not migration tracking.

quiz.question: A company has a MySQL database on-premises and wants to migrate it to Amazon RDS MySQL with LEAST downtime. Which service should they use?
options: AWS DMS with Schema Conversion Tool
AWS DMS without Schema Conversion Tool
AWS SMS
AWS DataSync with MySQL agent
quiz.explanation: This is a homogeneous migration (MySQL → MySQL, same engine), so AWS DMS alone is sufficient — no Schema Conversion Tool is needed since the schemas are compatible. DMS keeps the source database operational during migration for minimal downtime. SCT is only required for heterogeneous migrations (different database engines). SMS migrates server workloads, not databases. DataSync transfers file system data, not live database replication.

quiz.question: A solutions architect must design a migration strategy for a large enterprise. The requirements include: (1) discovering all on-premises server dependencies, and (2) tracking the overall migration progress from a single view. Which TWO AWS services address these requirements? (Choose TWO)
options: AWS DMS
AWS Application Discovery Service
AWS Migration Hub
AWS SMS
Migration Evaluator
quiz.explanation: AWS Application Discovery Service discovers on-premises server dependencies (system config, performance, processes, network connections). AWS Migration Hub provides a single dashboard to track migration progress across tools. DMS and SMS execute database and server migrations respectively but do not provide the discovery or unified tracking functions described. Migration Evaluator builds financial business cases, not dependency discovery or progress tracking.

quiz.question: A company is decommissioning an on-premises Cassandra database and wants to move the data to Amazon DynamoDB. The schemas must be converted as part of the migration. Which AWS services are required?
options: AWS DMS only — it handles Cassandra-to-DynamoDB natively
AWS SCT to convert the schema, then AWS DMS to migrate data
AWS SMS to replicate the Cassandra server to AWS
AWS DataSync to copy Cassandra data files to DynamoDB
quiz.explanation: Cassandra to DynamoDB is a heterogeneous migration (different database technologies), requiring AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) to convert the schema first, then AWS DMS to migrate the data. DMS alone cannot convert schemas between incompatible database types. SMS migrates server workloads, not database schemas. DataSync transfers file system data, not NoSQL database records.

quiz.question: A startup uses an on-premises FTP server to receive daily transaction files from business partners. They want to migrate this to AWS with LEAST changes to their partners' workflows — partners should continue using FTP with the same hostname and credentials. Which service BEST meets this requirement with LEAST operational overhead?
options: An EC2 instance running an FTP server with Elastic IP
AWS Transfer Family with FTP protocol
Amazon S3 with presigned URL generation
AWS DataSync scheduled transfer
quiz.explanation: AWS Transfer Family supports FTP protocol (in addition to SFTP and FTPS), stores files in S3, integrates with existing authentication systems, and works with Route 53 so the hostname stays the same. This requires zero changes to partner workflows. An EC2-hosted FTP server has high operational overhead (OS maintenance, patching, availability). S3 presigned URLs require partners to change their workflow to HTTP/HTTPS. DataSync is for migrating existing data, not for receiving ongoing file transfers from partners.

quiz.question: A company is performing a database migration from Oracle to Amazon Aurora MySQL. Which TWO services are required for this heterogeneous migration? (Choose TWO)
options: AWS Application Discovery Service
AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT)
AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)
AWS Server Migration Service (SMS)
AWS Migration Hub
quiz.explanation: A heterogeneous migration (Oracle → Aurora MySQL = different engines) requires: (1) AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) to convert the Oracle schema to MySQL-compatible schema, and (2) AWS DMS to migrate the data while keeping the source Oracle database operational. Application Discovery Service is for server dependency discovery, not DB migration. SMS migrates server VMs, not databases. Migration Hub tracks progress but does not execute migrations.

lessons.title: AWS Analytics Services
lessons.overview: AWS provides a comprehensive analytics portfolio covering real-time streaming, interactive querying, big data processing, data warehousing, BI visualization, and data lake management. The exam tests your ability to select the right analytics service for the given data type, latency requirement, and architectural pattern.
lessons.overviewVi: AWS cung cấp danh mục analytics toàn diện bao gồm streaming real-time, truy vấn tương tác, xử lý big data, data warehouse, BI visualization và quản lý data lake. Kỳ thi kiểm tra khả năng chọn đúng dịch vụ analytics cho loại dữ liệu, yêu cầu độ trễ và mẫu kiến trúc cụ thể.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Analytics Service Categories
sections.contentHtml:
AWS analytics services are organized by data movement pattern and use case:

Real-time StreamingKinesis Data Streams, Kinesis Data Firehose, Kinesis Video Streams, Kinesis Data Analytics, Amazon MSK
Interactive / Ad-hoc QueryAmazon Athena (query S3 with SQL), Amazon Elasticsearch/OpenSearch
Big Data ProcessingAmazon EMR (Hadoop/Spark ecosystem)
Data Warehouse / OLAPAmazon Redshift + Redshift Spectrum
BI / VisualizationAmazon QuickSight
ETL / Data CatalogAWS Glue, AWS Data Pipeline
Data LakeAWS Lake Formation
Exam shortcut: Real-time + ordering → Kinesis Data Streams. Direct to S3/Redshift → Firehose. Ad-hoc S3 query → Athena. Hadoop/Spark → EMR. Warehouse → Redshift. Dashboards → QuickSight.
sections.contentVi: Dịch vụ analytics AWS được tổ chức theo mẫu di chuyển dữ liệu và ứng dụng: Streaming real-time — Kinesis Data Streams, Firehose, Video Streams, Data Analytics, MSK; Truy vấn tương tác/ad-hoc — Athena (query S3 bằng SQL), Elasticsearch/OpenSearch; Xử lý big data — EMR (hệ sinh thái Hadoop/Spark); Data Warehouse/OLAP — Redshift + Redshift Spectrum; BI/Visualization — QuickSight; ETL/Data Catalog — Glue, Data Pipeline; Data Lake — Lake Formation. Gợi nhớ thi: real-time + thứ tự → Kinesis Data Streams; trực tiếp lên S3/Redshift → Firehose; ad-hoc query S3 → Athena; Hadoop/Spark → EMR; warehouse → Redshift; dashboard → QuickSight.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Amazon Kinesis Family
sections.contentHtml:
The Kinesis family handles real-time data streaming workloads:

Kinesis Data StreamsMassively scalable real-time data streaming; millisecond latency; ordered records; multiple consumers can read concurrently; use cases: clickstreams, IoT telemetry, financial transactions, leaderboards
Kinesis Data FirehoseFully managed; delivers directly to S3, Redshift, Elasticsearch, HTTP endpoints; no custom consumer needed; can transform data via Lambda before delivery
Kinesis Video StreamsStream video from connected devices for analytics, ML, and playback; auto-provisions infrastructure; stores/encrypts/indexes video
Kinesis Data AnalyticsServerless; analyze streaming data in real-time using SQL or Apache Flink (Java/Scala); delivers results via Firehose to S3/Redshift
Kinesis Data Streams vs SQS: SQS Standard has no ordering guarantee; SQS FIFO has ordering but limited TPS; SQS is not real-time. Kinesis Data Streams = real-time + ordered + multiple concurrent consumers.
sections.contentVi: Kinesis Data Streams là streaming dữ liệu real-time quy mô lớn với độ trễ mili giây, bản ghi có thứ tự, nhiều consumer đọc đồng thời; dùng cho clickstream, IoT, giao dịch tài chính, leaderboard. Kinesis Data Firehose được quản lý hoàn toàn, phân phối trực tiếp đến S3, Redshift, Elasticsearch, HTTP endpoint, không cần consumer tùy chỉnh, có thể biến đổi dữ liệu qua Lambda. Kinesis Video Streams truyền video từ thiết bị kết nối cho analytics, ML và phát lại, tự cung cấp hạ tầng, lưu trữ/mã hóa/lập chỉ mục video. Kinesis Data Analytics serverless phân tích streaming real-time bằng SQL hoặc Apache Flink (Java/Scala), phân phối kết quả qua Firehose. So sánh với SQS: SQS Standard không đảm bảo thứ tự; SQS FIFO có thứ tự nhưng TPS hạn chế; SQS không real-time. Kinesis Data Streams = real-time + có thứ tự + nhiều consumer đồng thời.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: Query, Processing, and Warehouse Services
sections.contentHtml:
Interactive Query

Amazon AthenaServerless interactive SQL query service for S3 data; pay per query; integrates with Glue Data Catalog and QuickSight; best for ad-hoc queries on S3 data lake
Amazon OpenSearch (Elasticsearch)Managed Elasticsearch; supports ELK stack; full-text search; schemaless JSON; pay only for use

Big Data Processing

Amazon EMRElastic MapReduce; big data framework platform; supports Hadoop, Spark, Flink, HBase, Hive, Presto; runs on EC2/EKS/Outposts; NOT serverless; deploy in VPC; can access S3 and DynamoDB

Data Warehouse

Amazon RedshiftFast data warehouse; columnar storage + massively parallel query; primarily OLAP; runs on EC2 (NOT serverless); petabytes in Redshift + exabytes via S3 through Redshift Spectrum
Redshift SpectrumQuery S3 data directly without loading it into Redshift — extends warehouse queries to the S3 data lake
EMR vs Redshift: EMR = big data framework (Hadoop/Spark jobs); Redshift = SQL data warehouse for BI queries. Both run on EC2 (neither is serverless).
sections.contentVi: Truy vấn tương tác: Amazon Athena là dịch vụ query SQL serverless cho dữ liệu S3, trả phí theo query, tích hợp Glue Data Catalog và QuickSight, tốt nhất cho ad-hoc query trên data lake S3. Amazon OpenSearch (Elasticsearch) là Elasticsearch được quản lý, hỗ trợ ELK stack, tìm kiếm toàn văn bản, JSON không schema. Xử lý big data: Amazon EMR (Elastic MapReduce) là nền tảng big data hỗ trợ Hadoop, Spark, Flink, HBase, Hive, Presto, chạy trên EC2/EKS/Outposts, KHÔNG serverless, deploy trong VPC. Data Warehouse: Amazon Redshift là data warehouse nhanh, lưu trữ columnar + truy vấn song song, chủ yếu OLAP, chạy trên EC2 (KHÔNG serverless), petabytes trong Redshift + exabytes qua S3 thông qua Redshift Spectrum. Redshift Spectrum cho phép query dữ liệu S3 trực tiếp mà không cần nạp vào Redshift. EMR vs Redshift: EMR = nền tảng big data (Hadoop/Spark); Redshift = SQL data warehouse cho BI.

sections.label: Core Concept
sections.heading: ETL, Data Catalog, and Data Lake Services
sections.contentHtml:
ETL and Data Movement

AWS GlueServerless ETL service; Data Catalog discovers and stores metadata from S3 and other sources; auto-discovers data schemas; immediately searchable/queryable once cataloged
AWS Data PipelineMoves and processes data between AWS compute and storage services on scheduled intervals; transfers to/from on-premises; supports S3, RDS, DynamoDB, EMR

Streaming Kafka

Amazon MSKManaged Streaming for Apache Kafka; fully managed Kafka; real-time streaming pipelines using Kafka APIs; populate S3 data lakes; power ML/analytics

Data Lake

AWS Lake FormationSimplifies secure data lake setup on S3; creates data catalogs; collects/catalogs from multiple sources; classifies data using ML; secures sensitive data; queryable via Athena, Redshift, EMR

BI / Visualization

Amazon QuickSightServerless BI service; interactive dashboards for browser/mobile; embeddable; ML-powered insights; scales to thousands of users
Amazon CloudSearchManaged search service for implementing search in applications; facet info, autocomplete, geospatial; provisions servers automatically
sections.contentVi: ETL và di chuyển dữ liệu: AWS Glue là dịch vụ ETL serverless, Data Catalog tự khám phá và lưu metadata từ S3 và các nguồn khác, dữ liệu được lập chỉ mục có thể tìm kiếm/query ngay sau khi catalog. AWS Data Pipeline di chuyển và xử lý dữ liệu giữa các dịch vụ AWS theo lịch, hỗ trợ on-premises, S3, RDS, DynamoDB, EMR. Streaming Kafka: Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Kafka) là Kafka được quản lý hoàn toàn, xây dựng pipeline streaming real-time dùng Kafka API, nạp data lake S3. Data Lake: AWS Lake Formation đơn giản hóa thiết lập data lake bảo mật trên S3, tạo data catalog, thu thập từ nhiều nguồn, phân loại dữ liệu bằng ML, bảo mật dữ liệu nhạy cảm, có thể query qua Athena/Redshift/EMR. BI/Visualization: Amazon QuickSight là BI serverless, dashboard tương tác trên browser/mobile, có thể nhúng vào ứng dụng, ML-powered, mở rộng đến hàng nghìn người dùng. Amazon CloudSearch là dịch vụ tìm kiếm được quản lý cho ứng dụng, hỗ trợ facet, autocomplete, tìm kiếm địa lý.

sections.label: Deep Dive
sections.heading: Kinesis Data Streams vs SQS vs Firehose
sections.contentHtml:
These three are frequently compared in exam questions:

Kinesis Data StreamsReal-time (ms latency); ordered records; multiple consumers; requires custom consumer code; data retained for replay; use: clickstreams, IoT, leaderboards requiring ordering
SQS StandardNot real-time (near-real-time); no ordering guarantee; one consumer per message (message deleted after read); decoupling, queuing
SQS FIFOOrdering guaranteed; limited to 3,000 TPS; one consumer per message
Kinesis Data FirehoseFully managed delivery to S3/Redshift/ES; no custom consumer; data NOT retained; ~60 second buffer minimum
Key distinction: "Real-time" + "ordering of records" + "multiple concurrent consumers" → Kinesis Data Streams. "Deliver streaming data to S3" → Firehose. "Decouple application components" → SQS.
sections.contentVi: So sánh ba dịch vụ thường xuất hiện trong câu hỏi thi: Kinesis Data Streams — real-time (ms latency), bản ghi có thứ tự, nhiều consumer đọc đồng thời, cần code consumer tùy chỉnh, dữ liệu được giữ lại để replay; dùng cho clickstream, IoT, leaderboard cần thứ tự. SQS Standard — không real-time (gần real-time), không đảm bảo thứ tự, mỗi message chỉ một consumer đọc (xóa sau khi đọc); dùng để tách rời các thành phần ứng dụng. SQS FIFO — đảm bảo thứ tự, giới hạn 3,000 TPS, một consumer mỗi message. Kinesis Data Firehose — phân phối được quản lý đến S3/Redshift/ES, không cần consumer tùy chỉnh, dữ liệu KHÔNG được lưu giữ, buffer tối thiểu ~60 giây. Phân biệt chính: 'real-time' + 'thứ tự bản ghi' + 'nhiều consumer đồng thời' → Kinesis Data Streams; 'phân phối streaming đến S3' → Firehose; 'tách rời ứng dụng' → SQS.

sections.label: Exam Focus
sections.heading: Analytics Service Selection Triggers
sections.contentHtml:
Map these keywords to the correct analytics service:

  • Real-time streaming + ordering of records + multiple consumers → Kinesis Data Streams
  • Stream data directly to S3 / Redshift / Elasticsearch → Kinesis Data Firehose
  • Analyze streaming data with SQL or Flink → Kinesis Data Analytics
  • Stream video from IoT / cameras → Kinesis Video Streams
  • Kafka-based streaming pipelines → Amazon MSK
  • Ad-hoc SQL queries on S3 data / serverless / pay-per-query → Amazon Athena
  • Full-text search / ELK stack / JSON search → Amazon OpenSearch (Elasticsearch)
  • Hadoop / Spark / Hive / big data framework jobs → Amazon EMR
  • Fast SQL data warehouse / OLAP / columnar storage → Amazon Redshift
  • Query S3 without loading into Redshift → Redshift Spectrum
  • Serverless ETL / Data Catalog / schema discovery → AWS Glue
  • BI dashboards / business visualization / embed dashboards → Amazon QuickSight
  • Secure data lake setup on S3 → AWS Lake Formation
Serverless vs not: Serverless = Athena, Glue, QuickSight, Kinesis Data Analytics. NOT serverless (EC2-based) = EMR, Redshift.
sections.contentVi: Khớp từ khóa với dịch vụ analytics: Streaming real-time + thứ tự + nhiều consumer → Kinesis Data Streams; Stream dữ liệu trực tiếp đến S3/Redshift/ES → Firehose; Phân tích streaming bằng SQL/Flink → Kinesis Data Analytics; Stream video từ IoT/camera → Kinesis Video Streams; Pipeline Kafka → Amazon MSK; Query SQL ad-hoc trên S3/serverless/trả phí theo query → Athena; Tìm kiếm toàn văn/ELK/JSON → OpenSearch (Elasticsearch); Hadoop/Spark/Hive/big data → EMR; Data warehouse SQL nhanh/OLAP/columnar → Redshift; Query S3 không nạp vào Redshift → Redshift Spectrum; ETL serverless/Data Catalog/khám phá schema → Glue; Dashboard BI/visualization → QuickSight; Thiết lập data lake bảo mật trên S3 → Lake Formation. Serverless vs không: serverless = Athena, Glue, QuickSight, Kinesis Data Analytics; KHÔNG serverless (EC2) = EMR, Redshift.

summary: Kinesis Data Streams = real-time ordered streaming with multiple consumers (ms latency); Firehose = fully managed delivery to S3/Redshift/ES with no custom consumer required
Athena is a serverless pay-per-query SQL service for S3 data lakes; EMR runs Hadoop/Spark on EC2 for big data framework jobs (not serverless)
Redshift is an EC2-based columnar data warehouse for OLAP; Redshift Spectrum extends Redshift queries to S3 without loading data
AWS Glue provides serverless ETL and a Data Catalog that auto-discovers schemas from S3; Lake Formation builds secure data lakes on S3 queryable by Athena/Redshift/EMR
QuickSight is serverless BI for interactive dashboards; MSK is managed Kafka for teams that need Kafka APIs for streaming pipelines
summaryVi: Kinesis Data Streams = streaming real-time có thứ tự với nhiều consumer (ms latency); Firehose = phân phối được quản lý đến S3/Redshift/ES không cần consumer tùy chỉnh
Athena là SQL serverless trả phí theo query cho data lake S3; EMR chạy Hadoop/Spark trên EC2 cho big data (không serverless)
Redshift là data warehouse columnar EC2-based cho OLAP; Redshift Spectrum mở rộng query Redshift sang S3 không cần nạp dữ liệu
AWS Glue cung cấp ETL serverless và Data Catalog tự khám phá schema từ S3; Lake Formation xây data lake bảo mật trên S3 queryable qua Athena/Redshift/EMR
QuickSight là BI serverless cho dashboard tương tác; MSK là Kafka được quản lý cho pipeline streaming dùng Kafka API

quiz.question: A gaming company needs to process millions of player score events per second in real-time, maintain the order of events per player, and allow multiple downstream analytics services to consume the same stream simultaneously. Which AWS service BEST meets these requirements?
options: Amazon SQS FIFO
Amazon SQS Standard
Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose
quiz.explanation: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams provides real-time (millisecond latency) ordered data streaming with support for multiple concurrent consumers reading the same stream. SQS FIFO provides ordering but has limited TPS (3,000) and does not support multiple concurrent consumers of the same message. SQS Standard has no ordering guarantee. Kinesis Data Firehose delivers to destinations like S3 but does not support custom multiple consumers or record ordering.

quiz.question: A company wants to ingest clickstream data from their website and automatically deliver it to Amazon S3 for long-term storage, with the LEAST operational overhead. No real-time processing is needed — just reliable delivery to S3. Which service is MOST appropriate?
options: Kinesis Data Streams with a custom Lambda consumer
Kinesis Data Firehose
Amazon SQS with an EC2 consumer writing to S3
Amazon MSK with a Kafka consumer
quiz.explanation: Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose is fully managed and delivers streaming data directly to S3 (and other destinations) without requiring any custom consumer code — minimal operational overhead. Kinesis Data Streams requires writing and managing custom consumer code. SQS with EC2 requires managing both the EC2 instances and consumer code. MSK requires managing Kafka and custom consumer applications.

quiz.question: A data analyst needs to run ad-hoc SQL queries against a large dataset stored in Amazon S3 without setting up any servers or loading data into a database. The solution must be MOST cost-effective with pay-per-query pricing. Which service should they use?
options: Amazon Redshift
Amazon EMR with Hive
Amazon Athena
Amazon RDS
quiz.explanation: Amazon Athena is a serverless interactive query service that queries data directly in S3 using standard SQL, charging only per query with no infrastructure to manage. Redshift requires provisioning a cluster and loading data into it. EMR requires provisioning an EC2 cluster and managing Hive — high operational overhead. RDS is a relational database service for OLTP workloads, not S3-based analytics.

quiz.question: A large financial institution runs complex quarterly analytical reports on petabytes of historical transaction data. They need the FASTEST query performance with columnar storage and massively parallel processing. Which service is MOST appropriate?
options: Amazon Athena
Amazon DynamoDB
Amazon EMR with Spark
Amazon Redshift
quiz.explanation: Amazon Redshift is a purpose-built data warehouse using columnar storage and massively parallel query execution, designed for OLAP workloads on petabytes of structured data with fast query performance. Athena is serverless and suitable for ad-hoc queries but not optimized for the fastest OLAP performance on petabytes. DynamoDB is a NoSQL key-value store, not a data warehouse. EMR with Spark is for big data processing jobs, not SQL data warehousing.

quiz.question: A company stores exabytes of historical data in Amazon S3. Their data warehouse team wants to run SQL queries joining their Redshift warehouse data with the S3 historical data without loading the S3 data into Redshift. Which capability enables this?
options: Amazon Athena federated query
AWS Glue ETL job loading S3 into Redshift
Amazon Redshift Spectrum
Amazon EMR with Hive accessing S3
quiz.explanation: Amazon Redshift Spectrum allows Redshift to query data in S3 directly without loading it into the Redshift cluster — it extends Redshift SQL queries to the S3 data lake. Athena can query S3 but cannot join with Redshift tables in a single query. Glue ETL would require loading data into Redshift, defeating the purpose. EMR with Hive can access S3 but is not the same as extending Redshift's warehouse queries.

quiz.question: A DevOps team ingests application logs into Amazon S3 and needs to run complex big data processing jobs using Apache Spark to analyze failure patterns. The jobs run on a scheduled basis. Which service is MOST appropriate?
options: Amazon Athena
Amazon Redshift
Amazon EMR
AWS Glue
quiz.explanation: Amazon EMR (Elastic MapReduce) is a managed big data framework platform that supports Apache Spark (along with Hadoop, Flink, Hive, etc.) for running complex processing jobs on large datasets in S3. Athena is for interactive SQL queries, not Spark jobs. Redshift is a SQL data warehouse, not a Spark execution environment. Glue is a serverless ETL service but is designed for ETL pipelines, not arbitrary Spark big data jobs.

quiz.question: A business intelligence team needs to create interactive dashboards that can be embedded in a customer-facing web portal and shared with thousands of end users without provisioning BI servers. Which AWS service provides this with LEAST operational overhead?
options: Amazon EMR with Jupyter notebooks
Amazon Redshift with a BI tool on EC2
Amazon QuickSight
Kibana on Amazon OpenSearch
quiz.explanation: Amazon QuickSight is a serverless BI service that provides interactive dashboards, supports embedding in web applications, scales to thousands of users without server management, and includes ML-powered insights. EMR with Jupyter requires EC2 infrastructure management. Redshift + EC2 BI tool requires managing both the warehouse and BI server. Kibana on OpenSearch is for log/search analytics dashboards, not general BI embedding.

quiz.question: A data engineering team needs a serverless ETL service that can automatically discover data schemas from Amazon S3, maintain a central metadata catalog, and make the data immediately queryable by Athena. Which service should they use?
options: AWS Data Pipeline
Amazon EMR with Hive Metastore
AWS Glue
AWS Lake Formation
quiz.explanation: AWS Glue provides a serverless ETL service with a Data Catalog that automatically discovers and stores metadata (schemas) from S3 and other sources. Once cataloged, data is immediately searchable and queryable by Athena. Data Pipeline schedules data movement but is not a serverless ETL service with schema discovery. EMR with Hive Metastore requires provisioning EC2 clusters. Lake Formation builds a data lake on top of Glue's Data Catalog but does not replace the ETL and schema discovery function.

quiz.question: A security team wants to implement a centralized log analytics platform using the ELK (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) stack to search and visualize application logs with full-text search capabilities. Which AWS service is MOST appropriate for the Elasticsearch component?
options: Amazon Athena
Amazon Redshift
Amazon OpenSearch Service (formerly Elasticsearch Service)
Amazon Kinesis Data Analytics
quiz.explanation: Amazon OpenSearch Service (formerly Amazon Elasticsearch Service) is a managed Elasticsearch service that supports the ELK stack, full-text search, schemaless JSON, and Kibana for visualization — exactly what the ELK architecture requires. Athena queries structured S3 data with SQL, not full-text log search. Redshift is a columnar data warehouse, not a full-text search engine. Kinesis Data Analytics analyzes real-time streams, not static log storage.

quiz.question: A streaming media company needs to ingest video feeds from thousands of IoT cameras, store the video, and run ML-based anomaly detection on the footage. Which Kinesis service is SPECIFICALLY designed for this use case?
options: Kinesis Data Streams
Kinesis Data Firehose
Kinesis Video Streams
Kinesis Data Analytics
quiz.explanation: Amazon Kinesis Video Streams is specifically designed to stream video from connected devices, automatically provision infrastructure, and store/encrypt/index video for analytics, ML, and playback. Kinesis Data Streams handles record-level event streaming, not video binary streams. Kinesis Data Firehose delivers data to storage destinations. Kinesis Data Analytics runs SQL/Flink queries on data streams, not video.

quiz.question: A company is setting up a data lake on Amazon S3 from multiple sources (RDS, DynamoDB, on-premises systems). They need to enforce fine-grained access controls on the data and make it queryable by Athena, Redshift, and EMR with LEAST setup complexity. Which service simplifies this?
options: AWS Glue Data Catalog with IAM policies
Amazon S3 bucket policies with manual schema setup
AWS Lake Formation
AWS Data Pipeline
quiz.explanation: AWS Lake Formation simplifies secure data lake setup on S3 by creating data catalogs from multiple sources, classifying data using ML, enforcing fine-grained access controls, and making data immediately queryable by Athena, Redshift, and EMR. Glue Data Catalog is used by Lake Formation but requires more manual setup for security and access control. S3 bucket policies alone cannot provide table/column-level access control. Data Pipeline moves data but does not provide data lake governance.

quiz.question: A fintech company has existing Apache Kafka pipelines and wants to migrate to AWS with MINIMAL changes to their producer and consumer applications that use Kafka APIs. Which AWS service is MOST appropriate?
options: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose
Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka)
AWS EventBridge
quiz.explanation: Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka) is a fully managed Kafka service that uses native Kafka APIs — existing producers and consumers require minimal or no code changes. Kinesis Data Streams is a different streaming technology (not Kafka-compatible). Kinesis Data Firehose uses a different API and delivery model. EventBridge is an event bus for AWS service integration, not a Kafka-compatible streaming service.

quiz.question: A solutions architect needs to run real-time SQL analytics on a data stream of financial transactions and deliver the results to Amazon S3 every minute, without managing any servers. Which combination of services is MOST appropriate?
options: Kinesis Data Streams → EC2-hosted Spark Streaming → S3
Kinesis Data Streams → Kinesis Data Analytics (SQL) → Kinesis Data Firehose → S3
Amazon MSK → EMR Streaming → S3
SQS FIFO → Lambda SQL function → S3
quiz.explanation: Kinesis Data Analytics provides serverless real-time SQL analysis on streaming data, and Kinesis Data Firehose delivers the results to S3 on a scheduled buffer — the entire pipeline is serverless. An EC2 Spark Streaming setup has high operational overhead. MSK + EMR requires managing both Kafka and EC2 clusters. SQS FIFO + Lambda does not support continuous SQL stream analytics on high-throughput financial data.

quiz.question: A data team is designing a serverless analytics architecture on AWS. They need to (1) catalog and run ETL on raw S3 data, and (2) run ad-hoc SQL queries on the processed data in S3 without provisioning servers. Which TWO services should they use? (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon EMR
AWS Glue
Amazon Redshift
Amazon Athena
Amazon Kinesis Data Streams
quiz.explanation: AWS Glue provides serverless ETL and a Data Catalog that discovers schemas from S3 and prepares data. Amazon Athena provides serverless SQL queries directly on S3 data, integrating natively with the Glue Data Catalog. EMR runs big data jobs on EC2 — not serverless. Redshift is an EC2-based data warehouse requiring a cluster. Kinesis Data Streams is for real-time event streaming, not ETL or ad-hoc queries.

quiz.question: A company wants to move data between Amazon RDS, Amazon DynamoDB, and Amazon EMR on a scheduled nightly basis for data processing pipelines. Which AWS service is designed for this scheduled data movement?
options: AWS Glue
AWS Data Pipeline
Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose
AWS DataSync
quiz.explanation: AWS Data Pipeline is designed to move and process data between AWS compute and storage services (including RDS, DynamoDB, and EMR) on scheduled intervals. Glue is a serverless ETL service focused on schema discovery and transformation, not general scheduled data movement between compute services. Firehose handles streaming data delivery. DataSync is for migrating data from on-premises to AWS storage, not for scheduled pipelines between AWS services.

quiz.question: A solutions architect is evaluating streaming services for a use case that requires: (1) real-time processing with millisecond latency, and (2) guaranteed ordering of records per partition. Which TWO statements about the CORRECT service choice are true? (Choose TWO)
options: Amazon SQS Standard provides ordering guarantees per partition
Amazon Kinesis Data Streams provides millisecond latency
Amazon Kinesis Data Streams maintains ordered records within a shard
Amazon SQS FIFO supports unlimited throughput with strict ordering
Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose supports multiple concurrent consumers
quiz.explanation: Kinesis Data Streams provides millisecond (real-time) latency and maintains ordered records within a shard (partition). SQS Standard has no ordering guarantee. SQS FIFO guarantees ordering but is limited to 3,000 TPS — not unlimited. Kinesis Data Firehose delivers to storage destinations and does not support custom multiple concurrent consumers the way Kinesis Data Streams does.

quiz.question: An analytics team uses Amazon Redshift for their data warehouse but has 10 years of historical data (exabyte-scale) stored in Amazon S3 that is too expensive to load into Redshift. They want to include this historical S3 data in their Redshift SQL queries. Which capability allows this with LEAST cost?
options: Run EMR jobs to preprocess S3 data and load into Redshift nightly
Use AWS Glue to ETL S3 data into Redshift tables
Use Amazon Redshift Spectrum to query S3 in-place
Use Athena to query S3 and join with Redshift via federated query
quiz.explanation: Amazon Redshift Spectrum allows Redshift SQL queries to access S3 data directly in-place without loading it into Redshift — the query joins warehouse data with the S3 data seamlessly and charges only for S3 data scanned. This is the most cost-effective approach. Loading with EMR or Glue would incur S3-to-Redshift data transfer costs and Redshift storage costs. Athena federated query works differently and does not extend Redshift's native SQL to S3 the way Spectrum does.
